Africa adminstr., 24th Sept., 1892; orgnzd. printg. dept., and was apptd. editor of off. gazette, June, 1894; served as vol. in campaigns against Zarafl, etc., 1895 (red. Central African medal); won gold medal offered by Zomba rifle club, 1897, and the Edwards Cup, 1898; ch. Br. comsrr. for delimitation of Anglo-Portuguese boundary in B.C. Africa, 24th May, 1899; sec. to Sir H. Johnston's spec. miss. to Uganda, 24th Aug., 1899; passed exams. at Mid. Temp. and admitted a law student, 29th June, 1901; admitted to Gray's Inn, 1st July, 1901; sec. to Uganda adminstr., 1st Apr., 1901; apptd. judl. offr., 19th July, 1900; carried on duties of ch. judl. offr. and regist. in Uganda, 10th Apr., 1900, to 19th Dec., 1900; acted at headquarters for spec. comsrr. during his tour of inspection, 28th May to 8th June, 1900; and during the dep. comsrr.'s absence with Nandi expdt., 9th Jan. to 16th Feb., 1901; present post, sec. to Uganda adminstr.; regist. gen. of marriages, 1st Nov., 1902; joined Uganda vol. reserve, 1903; elected sec. of corps (company comdr.), 24th Jan., 1905; won annual championship and cup, 24th Mar., 1906; leader of African Prots. vola., Bisley, 1907; author of "Uganda and its Peoples," "Notes on the Anthropology and Ethnology of Indigenous Races," 1905.

CUNYNGHAME, SIR PERCY F., BART.—B. 1867; cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., July, 1886; asst. res. of Tuscan, May, 1890; res. (2nd class), Mar., 1896; lieut. 3rd battn. Middlesex Rifles, May, 1897; capt., Oct., 1901; ag. res., Limbang, May, 1899; res., 2nd class, Upper Rejang, Aug., 1901; commdt., Sarawak Rangers, July, 1903; res., 1st div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advocate, sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advocate high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel to Imperial govt. in arbit. with late S.A.R. re Asiatic question, 1885; ag. judge of high ct. (late S.A.R.), 15th Apr., 1890, to 31st Jan., 1900; pres. of crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, 2nd Nov., 1890, to 31st Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Sept., 1903.

CURLING, CLARENCE NAPIER.—B. 1868; served under educn. dept., Cape Colony, 1880-89; B.S.A. pol., 1890-91; educn. dept., 1892-93; headm. of diocesan gram. schl., Nassau, Bahamas, 1894-95; res. just. and collr. of Nassau, Bahamas, Aug., 1895; ch. regist.r., G. Co., 12th Jan., 1899; dist. comsrr., Mar., 1899; regist. and sheriff, 1901; prov. comsrr., Jan., 1901.
CURMI, TANGRED, M.V.O. (1907).—Asst. sec. of pol. Malta, 1890; senr. asst., 1902; supt., 1907.

CURRIE, SIR DONALD, G.C.M.G. (1877).—K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1825; char. of the Union-Castle Mail Packets Co. In July, 1876, at the time of the negotiations between the Earl of Carnarvon and Pres. Brand, he assisted by his good offices in bringing about the success of these negotiations, and thus ending the long dispute between the Br. Govt. and the Orange Free State in reference to the diamond fields; formerly M.P. for Perthshire.

CURRIE, HARRY AUGUSTUS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. at Glenalmond and Cooper's Hill; Fell. of Cooper's Hill, 1887; on Fort Bridge constn. wks., 1888; asst. engr., pub. wks. dept., India, 1888-1892; asst. man., N.W. rly., India, 1892-94; dep. consulting engr. for rlys., 1894-95; asst. sec. to govt. of India, 1894-97; dep. consulting engr. for rlys., 1897-98; lent to Pekin Syndicate for employment in China, 1898-1900; dep. man., Oude and Rohilkund rly.; man., Uganda rly., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns., E. Africa Prot., 1907.

CUSACK-SMITH, SIR BERRY, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1839; ed. at Eton; barrister, Middle Temp., 1884; law scholarship, 1883; formerly lieut. V.B. Essex regt.; apptd. H.M. Consul-Samoa, Jan., 1890, and H.M. dep. comsrr. for the W Pacific in Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curaco" to Union Group to report on land claims, Aug., 1892; dep. comsrr. for the Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of res. in the Union Group, Oct., 1892; special judicial comsrr., Apr., 1893; apptd. to license and superintend employment of Gilbert Is. natives in Samos, Jan., 1894; consul-gen., Valparaiso, 1897.

CUSCADEN, W. A.—B. 1853; Sen. Sophister, Trin. Coll., Dub.; capt. 4th Roy. Dub. Fus.; nat. inspr. of constab., G. Coast, 1879; civil comsrr., Taecahau, 1881; ch. inspr. of pol., Singapore, 1883; asst. sup. of pol., Penang, 1884; ag. pol. sup., Malacca, 1889, and July, 1897; Singapore, 1891, and Apr., 1893; asst. sup. of pol., P.W., 1893; supt. pol., Malacca, July, 1897; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr.-gen. pol., S.S., Mar., 1901, to Aug., 1902, and Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904; supt. of pol., Sing., Aug., 1902; inspr.-gen. of pol., S.S., 18th Dec., 1906.
CUSSEN, HON. LEO FINN BERNARD.—B. 1859; judge of sup. ct., Victoria, since Mar., 1906.

D'AETH, JOHN, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jamaica, Jan., 1873; ch. ditto, Mar., 1875; dist. engr., W. dist., Jan., 1883; engr., Kingston dist., Aug., 1886; S. dist., Apr., 1892; Kingston and E. dist., Apr., 1899; seconded for service as col. engr. and surv.-gen., Dominica, Jan., 1901; asst. D.P.W., Jamaica, Mar., 1902; ag. D.P.W., 1902, 1903, 1905 and 1907.

DALE, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1867; ed. Greenwich and King's Coll., London; fell. soc. accnts. and auditors, fell. rev. statistical soc.; apptd. dir. customs Niger Coast Protec., Jan., 1895; asst. postmstr.-gen., Apr., 1896; ag. dir. gen. customs and postmstr.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1896, Oct., 1897, to June, 1898, Oct., 1899, to Feb., 1900; Dec., 1900, to July, 1901; employed reorganizing treasv., Oct., 1897; treas. Southern Nigeria, Feb., 1898; ag. sec., Feb. to Apr., 1900; Jan. to Apr., 1903, and Dec., 1905, to Feb., 1906; dir. pub. officers' guarantee fund, Apr., 1900; ag. treas., Lagos, Sept., 1904; financial comsrr. to combined admn. of Lagos and S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; and mem. of leg. and exec. couns.; ag. col. sec., 7th Jan. to 5th May, 1908; dep. gov., 7th Jan. to 5th May, 1908.

DALE, H. E.—B. 1875; ed. at St. Paul's Schol. and at Oxford; class. school, Balliol Coll., 1894; Hertford schol., 1895; Craven schol., 1895; Ireland schol., 1896; 1st class moda., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1898; B.A., 1898; Fell. New Coll., 1898; Derby schol., 1899; M.A., 1901; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct., 1898; sent on special mission to Br. Hond., Nov., 1903; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, Jan., 1907.

DALE, HAROLD ROSS.—B. 1861; entered atty.-gen.'s office, Cape, Dec., 1879; 2nd class clk., July, 1883; ch. clk. to solr.-gen., Grahamstown, Mar., 1885; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Nov., 1886; J.P. for the Colony; registr. of sup. ct. and taxing offr., 1st June, 1901.

DALLAS, FRANCIS HENRY.—Ed. Univ. Coll. School; asst. sec. Penang municipality, 1895; ag. sec., May, 1896; Sarawak civ. ser.; ag. editor Sarawak Gazette, and manager, Sarawak govt. printing office, 1897; ag. treas. and clk., supreme coun., June, 1902; treas. and recorder of sup. coun., 1903; mem. of comtce. of adminstr., 1903-4.

DALTON, THE REV. CANON JOHN NEALE, M.A., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1839; was gov. to T.R.H. Princes Albert Victor and George of Wales, and ag. chaplain to H.M.S. "Bacchante"; chaplain in ord. to Her Majesty, 1886; canon of Windsor, 1885.
DALY, SIR MALACHY BOWES, K.C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1836; Lieut.-Governor of Nova Scotia, 1890.
DALY, THE HON. THOMAS MAYNE, K.C.—Mem. for Selkirk, Manitoba, in Dominion house of commons; min. of interior, Canada, 1892-6.

DANAVALL, JOHN MARTIN.—Barrister, Mid Temp., 1877; ag. solr.-gen., Leeward Is., 1883; again in 1884; ag. mag. Dist. C, 1883, and again in 1885; 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., June, 1888; also dep.-lgl. of the vice-admiralty ct., 1889.

DANDY, JOSEPH HENRY.—Ed. Oakes Inst., Liverpool, certif. as inspr. of nuisances and sanitary inspr. from sanitary inst., 1896; 1st cl. advanced hygiene, S. Kensington, 1897; surveying (1st cl.) and certif. of sanitary knowledge, Victoria Univ., 1898; certif. schl. of hygiene, Univ. Coll., 1898; practical bacteriology, Univ. Coll., 1899; mem. of san. inst., 1900; san. inspr., Liverpool, chief sanitary inspr., Hong Kong, June, 1899.

DANE, ROBT., M.R.C.S., L.S.A. (1887), L.R.C.P. (1889).—House physician, Middlesex hosp., 1890; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, June, 1890; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1893; col. surg., Sing., Mar., 1901; ag. col. surg. res., Sing., May, 1905, to Jan., 1906; mem. of S. S. med. coun., July, 1905.

DANIEL, ROWLAND MORTIMER.—Ed. at St. Edmund's, Canterbury; passed civil ser. Lower Law (C.C.), 1907; 2nd lieut., Glam. artil. militia, 1893; certif. of musk., Rhyte; capt. and instr. of gunnery, 1898; seconded sub.-inspr., B.S.A. pol., Div. I. (now Bech. Prot. pol.), 1898; served through S. African war, 1900-02, including siege of Mafeking; ment. in desps.; brig. sig. offr. and staff capt., 1900-01; ag. asst. comsrr., S. Bech. Prot., 1902; asst. res. mag. at Serowe, 1903; ag. asst. comsrr., N. Prot., 1904; asst. comsrr., ditto, Jan., 1907.
DANIELL, HUMPHREY A.—Ed. at Eton; served with Hampshire regt. in S. Africa, 1901-02 (medal and 5 clasps); asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1906.

DANSEY, CLAUDE EDWARD MARJORIBANKS.—Ed. at Wellington; late lieut., Lancas. Fusiliers; capt., reserve of officers; served during Mata-beleland rebellion, 1896, with Matabele mounted pol. (medal); sub. comdt., B. N. Borneo constabulary, during operations against rebels under Mohamed Salich, May, 1899, to Jan., 1900 (medal); commanded squadron 1st African Light Horse during South African War, 1900-2 (2 medals and 6 clasps); staff-lieut. for intell. and A.D.C. to G.O.C., Harrismith dist., S. Africa, 1902-4; specially mentd. by gov. of B. N. Borneo for services during expedit. in interior, and mentd. in desps., S. Africa, 1900; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, 4th Nov., 1904; 2nd asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Aug., 1905.

DARBY, ARTHUR JOHN.—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1900 (medal and three clasps); asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, May, 1903; ag. P.L.S., 25th May to 27th July, 1905, and from 11th Apr., 1906, to 13th Feb., 1907; ag. sec., squatters' bd., 4th May, 1906, to 13th Feb., 1907; ag. land offr., Apr., 1907.

DARLEY, SIR FRED. MATTHEW, P.C. (1905). C.G.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1897), KT. BACH. (1887).—B. 1830; Ed. Dungannon Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., Hon. LL.D., T.C., 1903; barrister, Ireland, 1853, N. S. Wales, 1862, Queensland, 1886; Q.C., 1878; M.L.C., N. S. Wales, 1868-1886; vice-pres., exec. coun., N. S. Wales, 1881-2-3; ch. just., N. S. Wales, 1886; lieut.-gov., N. S. Wales, 1891; acted as such seven times for periods amounting in all to nearly four years, besides on twenty different occasions as deputy for different governors, amounting in all to over a year; apptd. one of the comsrs. to inquire into South African War, 1902.

DARNLEY, E. R.—B. 1875; scholar of Trim Coll., Camb., 1893; of Lond. Univ., 1897; B.A. (sixth wrangler) and B.Sc., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; asst. priv. sec. to Earl of Elgin, Jan., 1907; sec. to finance comtce., Jan., 1908.
DARTNELL, MAJOR-GEN. SIR J. G., K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1838; late of the 97th and 86th regts.; comdt. of the volr. and mounted pol., Natal, 1874; ret., 1903.
DASHWOOD, CHAS. JAMES, K.C. (1906).—Govt. res. and judge of the N. territory of S. Australia, 1892-1895; mem. H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1887-92; crown solr., 1905.

DAVID, PAUL AUGUST FELIX, B.A., Oxon.—Cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1896; asst. dist. offr. and treas., Kuala Lumpur, Perak, Dec., 1900; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Perak, Jan., 1903; dist. offr., Temerloh, Pahang, Mar., 1904; ditto, Christmas Is., May, 1907.

DAVIDSON, WALTER EDWARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; scholar and exhibitioner of Christ's Coll., Camb.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Aug., 1880; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1880; writer col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1881; transf'd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1881; attached to the Negombo pol. ct., May, 1882; acted as asst. agt., Negombo, July to Oct., 1882; extra office asst., Colombo Kachcheri, May, 1883; and ag. office asst., Nov., 1884; sec. to Ceylon commn., col. exhib., 1886; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Apr., 1888, Mar., 1892, and Feb., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Kalutara, Apr., 1899; Galle, Dec., 1890; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1887; Haldummulla, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1899; ag. govt. agt. and fiscal, prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1890; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Oct., 1896; N. Eliya, Feb., 1899; chmn. mun. coun. and mayor of Colombio, Apr., 1898; resig. as chmn. of mun. coun. on going on leave; asst. sec. to administrator, Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1902-3; gov., Seychelles, 15th Apr., 1904.

DAVIDSON-HOUSTON, LIEUT.-COL. WILFRED B.—Ed. at Corrig schl., Ireland, and St. Edward's, Oxford; 2nd lieut. 5th Royal Dublin Fus., Jan., 1887; capt., 1892; major, 1902; lieut.-col., 1906; B.S.A. Co. pol. and asst. consnr., Rhodesia, 1890-92; certificates, R.I.C. and Dublin met. pol., 1895; asst. inspr., G. Const Constaby, (Hausas), Jan., 1894; inspr., Jan., 1898; capt., W. A. F. F., Jan., 1898; on spec. serv. Kwahu and Ashanti-Akim, Feb., 1894, to Mar., 1895; Ashanti expdn., 1895-6, commanded advanced guard, main column (hon. ment. in desp. star); spec. miss. to N.W. Ashanti, Feb. to May, 1896; with Hausa detach. in London, Jubilee, 1897 (medal); operations in N. T., G. Coast, 1897-8 (medal and clasp); ag. res., Ashanti, May, 1899, to Apr., 1900; Ashanti campaign, 1900 (medal and clasp); S. African War, 1901-2; staff offr., Warrenton (medal and 5 clasps); adjutant 5th Roy. Duh. Fus., 1902; consnr. of Ashanti, 1902; ag. ch. consnr. of Ashanti, Mar. to May, 1903; and from July, 1904, to Mar., 1905; consnr. of Montserrat, 18th Mar., 1906; mem. gen. leg. and federal exec. couns., Leeward Is., 1906.

DAVIES, COLIN REES.—Ed. at Eton; admitted solr. of sup. ct., Sept., 1891; under-sheriff, Pembroke, 1897; called to the bar, Bahamas, July, 1901; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1903; ag. prosecuting coun. for the Crown, May, 1903; coroner, Nov., 1904; registr.-gen., Br. Hond., Apr., 1905; also registr., Vice-Admiralty ct.; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, and mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; also mem. of bds. of educn. and quarantine; J.P. for col.; atty.-gen., 5th Feb., 1907; joined Inner Ten., July, 1907; ag. ch. just. Mar. to Sept., 1908; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1908; chancellor of the diocese of B. Honduras and Cent. America.

DAVIES, HON. JOHN MARK.—M.L.C. for S. Yarra, Victoria, 1889; for Melbourne, 1899; min. for just., 1890-92; solr.-gen., 1899-1900, and 1902-3; min. of pub. instrn., 1903; atty.-gen. and solr.-gen., Victoria, 1903.

DAVIES, JOHN MURRAY.—B. 1878; ed. Xavier's Coll., and Melbourne Univ.; cad. Ceylon civ. serv., 1902; office asst., Galle, 1st pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1904; off. asst., dist., 1905; dist. judge, Kegalla, 1906; seconded to serv. as asst. land attitl. offr., 1906.

DAVIES, HON. SIR LOUIS HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1845; called to the bar, 1866; Q.C. in 1880; was one of the Canadian counsel before the internat. fishery commn. at Halifax in 1877; solr. gen. of P.E. Is., 1869, and again in 1872; led the opposition in P.E. Is. Legislature till Sept., 1876, when he became premier and atty.-gen.; resig. in Mar., 1879, and was defeated in the gen. elections which followed; was returned to the House of Commons at the gen. elections in 1882, and has since sat for Quebec; min. of marine and fisheries in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; mem. joint high comm. Quebec and Washington, 1898-9; puisne judge of sup. court, 1901.

DAVIES, THE HON. SIR MATTHEW HEAL KT. BACH. (1890).—B. 1850; solr.; late master of Prahran; mem. exec. coun. Victoria; min. without portfolio, Feb., 1886; speaker, legis. ass., 1887-92; vice-pres. and an exec. commr. in the centennial internat. exhib., 1888.

DAVIES, THEOPHILUS ALFRED.—Temp. cd. Sierra Leone, Jan., 1885; ag. asst. examin. of cust., Lavana, 1889; tide waiter, June, 1892; ag. asst. examin. offr. of cust., and postm. Mano Salija, Mar. to Nov., 1893; prev. offr., Bassia, 1895; ditto, Kukuna, 1896; on special mission to open cust. station at Saioniya, Nov., 1896; prev. offr., Saioniya, 1896 to 1902; insp. health, Saioniya, Tambakka dist., 1900; off. in charge of cust. and postmnr., Kambia, 1902, 1903; sub-collr. of cust., sub.-acctnt., postm. and money order clk., Mano Salija, July, 1903 to 1905; sen. warehouseman, Oct., 1905.

DAVIES, WM. REES.—Ed. Eton and Trs. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1885; called to bar (Inner Ten.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembroke; J.P. for Haverfordwest; M.P. for Pembroke, 1892-3; asst. priv. sec. to the late Sir W. V. Harcourt when chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5, and afterwards from 1895-8; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1897; administered the govt., June, 1901; ag. ch. just., May to Aug., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, 1902; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1902.

DAVIS, BECCLES.—Ed. C.M.S. grammar sch., Sierra Leone, and Fourah Bay Coll. (Dunelm); 1st class customs officer, 1894; ag. statistician, officer of customs, Niger Coast Prot., Apr. to Oct., 1894; P.C.O. at Opofo, Brass and Bonny, 1894-99; supervisor of customs, Southern Nigeria, 1899; transf'd. to pub. wks. dep., 1903; acctn., P.W.D., 1903; paymaster, P.W.D., 1904; ag. provincial paymaster, roads dept., E. Prov., Apr., 1908.

DAVIS, CHAS. GREY HOWICK.—B. 1871; Ent. col. ser. of Br. Guiana as asst. clk. aud. office, Nov., 1887; 5th cl. clk., Apr., 1889; 4th Sept., 1889; 3rd Jan., 1894; sec. to commr. of combined court on col. expenditure, 1894-5; 2nd cl. clk., Nov., 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., 1895-6; 7-8; ag. acctnt. to sup. ct., July, 1899; Sept., 1900; 1st clk. and acctnt., post office, Sept., 1900; ag. chrmn., poor law commn., 9th to 20th Dec., 1906, and from 16th Mar., 1907.
DAVIS, C. T. B. 1873; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar. Balliol Coll.

DAVIS, LEOPOLD ALBERT RICHARD.—B. 1862; permanent supernum. aid waiter, cust. dept., B. Guiana, Oct., 1882; 5th cl., Apr., 1883; 4th cl., Oct., 1884; 4th cl. offr. of cust., Jan., 1886; 3rd cl., Oct., 1887; 2nd cl., June, 1896; ag. 1st grade comsrs. of taxation and offr. asst. to comptroller of cust. in excise matters, July, 1898, to Mar., 1901; 1st cl. offr., 8th Sept., 1905; ag. survr. of cust., 1st Nov., 1905, to 28th Feb., 1906; 9th Aug. to 8th Nov., 1906; and from 1st Feb., 1907.

DAVIS, NICHOLAS DARNELL, C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1846; was for some years in Grenada, in the priv. sec.'s office; priv. sec. to Adminstr. Baynes for a short time. In Br. Guiana served as clerk in several depts.; Aug., 1870, to Feb., 1871, sec. to the comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imigrts.; sec. to roy. comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imigrts. in Mauritius, Feb., 1872, to Dec., 1873; comdt., Br. Sherbro, S. Leone, 1874; postmnr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1876; acted as rec-gen., Dec., 1880, to Sept., 1881; comptroller of customs, 1st Oct., 1881; ag. auditor-gen., mem. of the ct. of policy, and sen. comsrs. of the Vlissengen estate, 1882; mem. ct. of policy again in 1886, and since 1892 continuously; auditor-gen., 1st July, 1898; mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1898; has acted as govt. sec. on nine occasions, and three times as dep.-govt.

DAVIS, STEWART SPENCER, F.R.G.S.—Extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 4th ditto, 1895; 1st cl., treasy., 1898; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; acctnt., treasy., G. Coast, 1901; asst. treas., 1902; ag. ch. asst. treas., Dec., 1904; student, Gray's Inn, 1904.

DAVSON, CHARLES SIMON, B.A., LL.D., K.C.—Ed. at Westminster and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Tem., Jan., 1881; admitted to bar of Br. Guiana, 1882; acted as solr.-gen. on many occasions; stip. mag., 1888; solr.-gen., 1898; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1898, to Oct., 1899; again, Oct., 1900, to Apr., 1901, and on several other occasions; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1905.
DAVSON, SIR HENRY KATZ, Kt. Bach. (1903).—B. 1830; formerly mem. of ct. of policy, B. Guiana; dep. chmn. of W. India Comtee, since 1898.

DAWE, HON. CHAR.—Newfoundland; M.H.A., Harbour Grace, 1878-1889; for Port-de-Grave since 1898; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1894, and again in 1897; elected leader of opposition party in house of assem., 1906.

DAWE, HON. ELL.—B. 1843; M.H.A. for Harbour Grace, Newfoundland, 1889-1905; rlwy. comsrs., 1895; financial sec., 1893; chrmn., bd. of wks., 1895; min. of agric. and mines, 1900; mem. of cabinet, 1903; min. of marine and fisheries, 1904.
DAWSON, J. B. H.—M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., 1893, St. Mary's Hosp. med. schl., Lond.; dist. med. offr., Leeward Islds., Oct., 1894; dist. surg. of Sierra Leone, Aug., 1893.
DAWSON, JOHN WILDING.—Asst. eng. drainage dept., Mauritius, 30th Nov., 1894; drainage authority, 15th August, 1898; asst. dir. of pub. wks. and surveys, 28th Dec., 1904.
DAWSON, SAMUEL EDWARD, C.M.G. (1906).—King's printer, Canada, and controller of stationery, Nov., 1891; doc. of letters, Laval Univ., Quebec, and pres. of roy. soc. of Canada.

DAY, H. R. A.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. service, July, 1886; asst. res. Lundu, June, 1881; asst. res., Bitulu, June, 1893; res. 2nd cl., Mar., 1896; res. of Lundu, 1898; res., 2nd cl., Upper Sarawak, Jan., 1902.
DEACON, A.—Apptd. after exam. 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 1907.

DEACON, THOMAS.—Apptd., after open compet. exam., for the home civ. ser., to the Impl. Post Office, July, 1884; postmnr.-gen., Gold Coast Colony, Sept. 2nd, 1899; lieut., G. C. Rifle vol., 1899; capt., 13th Oct., 1901; ag. adjutant, 11th Apr., 1903.

DEAKIN, THE HON. ALFRED.—Comsrr. of pub. wks. and min. of water supply, Victoria, Mar. 8th, 1883; and also vice-pres. bd. of land and works; solr.-gen. Nov. 13th, 1883; pres. of roy. comsrs. on water supply, Dec., 1884; ch. sec. and min. of water supply, 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; sen. deleg. to col. confee., 1887; one of the Austr. delegates sent to Eng. in connection with Austr. federation bill; joined 1st ministry of the Commonwealth as atty.-gen. and min. of justice, Jan., 1901; prime min. of Australia, 1903-4, again prime min., 1905; represented Australia at Imp. Conf., 1907.

DEALY, THOMAS KIRKMAN, Inter. Arts (Lond.), D.A.F. (Paris), F.E.I.S., F.R.G.S., F.C.S., F.R. Hist. S., M.R. San. I., J.P.—B. 1869; ed. at St. Mary's Coll., Hammersmith; Marquis of Ripon's prize and a double first (head of each list), 1880; matric. (1st div.), Lond. Univ., 1883; 1st B.A. (1st div.), 1884; Diplome (degre superieur), 1902; et Societate perpetuel de l'Alliance Francaise (Paris); student of Lincoln's Inn; life mem. of Assoctn. Phonétique Internationale; mem. of the Malone Soc'y.; headmnr. St. Mary's, Derby, 1881-84; asst. master, govt. central schl., now Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1884; hon. examnr. in physics to Coll. of Medicine for Chinese, 1888; passed 2nd standard higher grade Chinese, Aug., 1890; ag. 2nd master, Victoria Coll., Apr., 1890, to Sept., 1891, Apr., 1894, to Apr., 1895, Mar., 1899, to Apr., 1900, May, 1900, to Oct., 1901, and Mar. to Sept., 1904; apptd. master in charge, pol. schl., Sept. 1st, 1900; resig. pol. schl., 30th Apr., 1906; apptd. 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 16th Mar., 1906; apptd. to bd. of examrs., 1906; ag. headmnr., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 8th Apr., 1908.

DE BOISSIERE, ARNAULD.—Entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1891; trooper, light horse, 1897; sub-lieut., 1901; lieut., 1903; ag. chief of fire brigade, Mar., 1903; J.P. for colony, and inspr. of Indian immigts.

DE BOISSIERE, RAOUL FERDINAND.—Ed. at St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad, schl. of med. of Royal Colls., and Univ. of Edin.; L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin.; L.F.P.S., Glasgow, 1901; prov. med. offr., Bua, Fiji, 1903; ditto, Tavuni, 1904; ditto, Nadroga, 1905; ditto, Kadavu, 1906; J.P., 1907; postmaster, Kadavu West, 1907; stip. mag. of the colony (acting at Kadavu), 1908.

DE CELLES, ALFRED DUCLOS, C.M.G. (1907), Chevalier de la Légion d'Honneur (1904).—B. 1843; editor of "Le Journal de Quebec," 1867 to 1872; editor of "La Minerve," Montreal, 1872 to 1880; asst. librarian of parlmt., Canada, 1880; joint librarian, 1885; author of "Papineau" and "Cartier," and "Les Etats-Unis, origines, constitution et développement."

DE FENZI, CHARLES WILLIAM PERKS DOUGLAS.—Clerk, ass't, and librarian, legis. coun., Natal, 5th Feb., 1889; sec. to comm., on stock importation, 1889-90; sec. to comm., on stock and garden diseases, 1894-5; clerk, of the legis. coun., on introduction of resp. govt., 10th Oct., 1893.
twin_text: DE FENZI, CHARLES WILLIAM PERKS DOUGLAS. Clk., ast. and librarian, legis. coun., Natal, 5th Feb., 1889; sec. to coun., on stock importation, 1889-90; sec. to, bot. gardens coun., 1891; sec. to coun., on scab and stock diseases, 1894-5; clk. of the legis. coun., on introduction of resp. govt., 10th Oct., 1893.

raw_text: DE FREITAS, ANTHONY, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. pol. mag., Grenada, 1900 and 1901; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1902; ag. regist., sup. ct., Grenada, 1902, to 1904; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, May to Oct., 1904; stip. mag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 4th Apr., 1907.
twin_text: DE FREITAS, ANTHONY, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. pol. mag., Grenada, 1900 and 1901; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1902; ag. regis., sup. ct., Grenada, 1902, to 1904; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, May to Oct., 1904; stip. mag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 4th Apr., 1907.

raw_text: DE GRUCHY, A. NICOLLE, M.B., C.M., Edin.—Asst. med. offr., and J.P., Victoria, Seychelles, and outlying islands, 11th Sept., 1901; col. surg., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1906.

DE HAMEL, HARGRAVE BARRY.—Major, Londonderry Artillery; asst. supt. of pol., Straits Settlements, June, 1897; seconded as ag. asst. commr. of pol., Kinta, Perak, Dec., 1905; supt. of pol., Penang, May, 1907.
twin_text: DE HAMEL, HARGRAVE BARRY, Major, Londonderry Artillery; astl. supt. of pol., Straits Stltnts., June, 1897; seconded as ag. astl. commr. of pol., Kunta, Perak, Dec., 1905; supt. of pol., Penang, May, 1907.

raw_text: DE KRETZER, EDWARD, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1854; employed in col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, 8th May, 1872; asst. and gen., 1st Jan., 1901; asst. cont. of rev., Mar., 1907.

DE LA CORNILLIERE, HENRI GASTON ERNEST, Draughtsman, P.W.D. of late O.F.S., Nov., 1892, to Mar., 1900; architectural asst., P.W.D., O.R.C., Mar., 1900, to July, 1902; head of architectural drawing off., ditto, July, 1902.

F.S.S.—B. 1845; entered the service in Mar., 1865; permanent under col. sec. of the col. of Cape of Good Hope, and perm. head of convicts and prisons dept., holding in addition the following offices: (1) accounting offr., col. sec.'s dept.; (2) controller printing and statuary; (3) mem. civ. ser. comsn.; (4) mem. tender bd.; (5) custodian stamps; (6) admstr. food and drugs act; (7) inspr. emigr. coolie ships. ELLIOTT, COL. JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B. (1877), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1824; entered R.N. n 1838; 2nd lieut., June, 1846; col., 1869; served in the China expdn., 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 1852 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; comld. the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla on the Irrawaddy (mentioned in gov.-gen.'s despatch; medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at the bombardment and surrender of the forts of Bomarsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the expdn. to Kertch and Yeni-Kale (medal with clasp, 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish medal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as maj. of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieut.-col., Devonshire Rifle Vol. Corps, 1870; in 1871 J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the bar, Mid. Ten., and joined the western circuit; in 1876 inspr. of prisons and stip. J.P., Br. Guiana; inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1881; adminstd. govt. of St. Vincent, 1884 and 1889; acted as col. sec., Barbados, 1886 and 1894; ret. 1895.

raw_text: FARAN, JOSEPH FRANCIS AMBROSE.—Ed. at Ushaw Coll., Durham; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised at Trinidad bar from 1877 to 1904; mem. of comsn. to inquire into difficulties existing between agricultural contractors and proprietors, and in collaboration with late Mr. Justice Lewis drafted "Agricultural Contracts Orde," 1889; retained on several occasions by the Crown to prosecute in criminal cases; mag., Ariaua, Trinidad, 16th Nov., 1904; ag. E. Is. and mainland; ret. from provincial govt., 1890; called to the senate of Canada, 1893; mem. of cabinet, without portfolio, 1894; ditto, 1896; resigned with premier in same year.

FIDDLES, GEORGE VANDELEUR, C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acctnt. to Sir A. Milner, high comsnr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C.O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907.

Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907.

raw_text: FISHER, HON. SYDNEY, B.A., P.C. (Brome).—B. 1830; ed. at High Schl. and McGill Univ. Montreal, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; unsuccessfully contested Brome for seat in H. of Commons Canada, 1880; elected for Brome at general elections, 1882 and 1887; defeated, 1891; again returned for Brome, 1896, and became min. of agric. in Sir Wilfrid Laurier's govt.; reelected by acclamation at general elections, 1900 and 1904; organised and directed management of Canadian section of Paris Exhibition, 1900; visited Japan to inquire into openings for Canadian trade, 1903.

raw_text: FITZGERALD, THE HON. GEORGE PARKER.—B. 1843; mem. of cabinet, Tasmania, without portfolio, 1888-92; while mem. of Tasmanian cabinet was mainly instrumental in obtaining construction of Strahan-Zeehan rly. connecting silver fields with deep water port; was also principal mover in the cause of technical educ.; also chmn. of tech. educ. comttee.; was (with Hon. Wm. Hart) app'd. liquidator of the Bank of Van Diemen's Land in Oct., 1891.

Scotia; govt. engr'n.-in-ch. inter-rly., 1863; engr'n.-in-ch., Canadian Pacific rly., 1871 to 1880; elected chancellor, Queen's Univ., Canada, 1880; re-elected 1883, and again 1886; deleg. from Canadian inst. and Amer. meteorological soc., N. raw_text: FORESTIER-WALKER, Gen. Sir Frederic William Edward Forestier, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1878), C.M.G. (1886), Knight of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1844; ed. at Sandhurst; entd. Scots. Guards, 1862; capt. and lieut.-col., 1875; col., 1878; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1902; served on staff in Kafir war, 1877-8; mil. sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, 1878-9; in Zulu war, 1879 (ment. in desps.); A.A. and Q.M.G., home district during war in Egypt, 1882; A.A. and Q.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1884; commdd. 2nd brig. at Aldershot, 1889-90; commdd. troops in Egypt, 1890-95; western dist., 1895-99; and troops in S. Africa, 1899; commdd. lines of communication in S. African war, 1899-1901; and troops in Cape Colony, 1900-1; gov. and commd-in-chief, Gibraltar 1st Aug., 1905.

FORGET, Amédée Emmanuel.—B. 1847; ed. Marieville Coll., Canada; called to the bar, 1871; served as sec. to council of the bar, Quebec; clk. of coun. and priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., N.W. Terr., 1876; ass't. comsrs. of Indian affairs for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1888; mem. of coun. of pub. instr., 1893; Indian comsrs., 1895; lieut.-gov., N.W.T., 11th Oct., 1898; lieut.-gov. of Saskatchewan, 1st Sept., 1905.

Terri., 1876; asst. comsrs. of Indian affairs for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1888; mem. of coun. of pub. instr., 1893; Indian comsrs., 1895; lieut.-gov., N.W.T., 11th Oct., 1898; lieut.-gov. of Saskatchewan, 1st Sept., 1905.

raw_text: FORREST, The Right Hon. Sir John, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1891), LL.D. (Cumb., 1897), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1847; Hon. Fellow of the Italian geog. soc'y.; Hon. Fellow of the imp. geo. societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey dept. of W. Australia, 1865; commanded exploring expeds. in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichhardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the S. Coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the W. coast; to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received thanks of gov. and legis. coun., and gold medal of the Roy. Geog. Soc'y. of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; dep. survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay district and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in N.W. Australia, 1878; acted as comsrs. of crown lands and survr.-gen.; comsrs. of crown lands and survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1883 to Dec., 1890, during which time was mem. of the exec. and legis. couns.; in Mar., 1883 and 1886, reported on the Kimberley dist., N. Australia, and selected the towns, etc., of Wyndham in Cambridge Gulf; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible govt., in which he took the position of premier and treas., and which he continuously held for over 10 yrs., resigning on 13th Feb., 1902, to join the 1st Commonwealth Govt. of Australia; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian federation convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1875, "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; deleg. to col. confe., 1887; represented W. Australia at the federal convention at Adelaide, Mar., 1897, and in June of the same year was present as rep. of the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; again rep. W. Australia at fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; Melbourne, 1898; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as postmaster-general, Jan., 1901; defence minister, 1901 to 1903; and min. for home affairs until Apr., 1904; treas. of Commonwealth, July, 1905; resig., July, 1907.

raw_text: FOSBERY, Widnham Francis Widnham, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1869; cons. agt., Niger Coast Prot., 1893; ag. vice-coms., 1894-5; dist. comsrs., 1896; ag. div. comsrs., 1897-8; pol. offr., centra division expdn., 1898 (severely wounded); res. Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Benin Terr. expdn., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comsrs., 1900; pol. offr., Ishan expdn., 1901 (medal with clasp). div. commr., 1902; pol. offr., Asaba Hint. expedit., 1902; senior div. commr., 1903; pol. offr., Igarra expedit., 1903 (clasp); ag. high commr., 1903-4; ag. sec. to adminstr., 1904; dep. high commr., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and again in Sept., 1905; prov. commr., 1906; ag. col. sec., S. Nigeria, May to June, 1906; ag. gov., June to Aug., 1906; dep. gov. and ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1906.

FOSTER, THE HON. GEO. E., B.A., D.C.L.—Ed. common and superior schls., King's County, New Bruns.; Douglas gold medallist, Univ. N.B., 1866; B.A., 1868; medallist, ancient rhetoric, Edin., 1873; prof. classics, Univ. N.B., 1873-9; returned to parlmt., 1882; min. of marine and fisheries for Dominion, Dec., 1885; min. of finance, 1888 to 1896; represented Canada at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894.

FOSTER, HON. RICHARD WITTY.—Mem. of H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1893 to 1906; Commr. of pub. wks., 1899; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904; ditto, and min. of agric., 1st March to 26th July, 1905.
FOSTER, EDMUND CHRISTOPHER.—L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); col. surg., prov. Wellesley (South), S. Sttlnts., 26th Aug., 1891; supt. S. S. emig. depot, Negapatam, 17th Nov., 1900.

FOWLIS, HON. GEORGE—B. 1860; ed. at Waterside schl., Ayrshire; mem. of H. of R. for Auckland City, N.Z., 1899, and for Grey Lynn (Auckland) since 1902; chmn. of Congregational Union of N.Z. in 1899; min. of educn., and pub. health, and min. in charge of hosp., and charitable aid depts., from 6th Aug., 1906.

FOWLER, FRANK.—B. 1864; asst. clk., pub. wks. dept., Brit. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clk., Jan., 1881; 2nd clk., Apr., 1884; 1st clk. and draftsman, govt. id. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd govt. surv., May, 1886; senr. govt. surv., Apr., 1896; apptd. J.P., Nov., 1900; asst. commr. of lands and mines, Apr., 1902; commr. of lands and mines, 1st Apr., 1903; chmn., Shanks Canal commrs., Mar., 1907; deputy chmn., local govt. bd., Dec., 1907; mem. of local govt. bd.; mem. of bd. of examrs. for land survs.; mem. of bd. of agric.; has held numerous acting appointments.

FOWLER, GEORGE MERRICK, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1852; ed. at Chel. Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., 5th Jan., 1874; ag. asst. collr. of cust., Jaffna, 1st July, 1876; offr. of cls. V., 1st Sept., 1876; ag. landing and tide surv., customs, Galle, 25th Sept., 1877; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, 1st Feb., 1878; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Feb., 1878; ag. off. asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., 10th Mar., 1882; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, 1st Apr., 1883; ag. dist. judge, Chilau, 21st Oct., 1883; ag. pol. mag., Galle, 10th Oct., 1884; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, 8th Jan., 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya-Vilankulam and Mullaitivu, 1st Feb., 1886; offr. of cls. III., 1st May, 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, 15th Apr., 1891; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 7th Dec., 1894; offr. of cls. II., 16th Dec., 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1897; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., 5th July, 1897; ag. dist. judge, Negombo, 23rd Oct., 1897; govt. agt., Ratnapura, 23rd June, 1898; ag. govt. agt., W. prov., 1st June, 1899; offr. of cls. I., 1st Jan., 1900; govt. agt., W. prov., 10th Feb., 1902; ag. and gen., 14th Apr., 1905; ag. col. sec., 8th Sept., 1905; ag. aud. gen., 3rd Dec., 1905; ag. col. sec., 5th Dec., 1906; controller of rev., 18th May, 1907; ag. col. sec., 11th July, 1907; ret., Sept., 1907.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judicial dept., Gambia, 1890; gov't. offf., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-05; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

raw_text: FOXON, CUTHBERT COLENSO.—B. 1867; clk. and Zulu interp. to asst. commr. and res. mag. Ndawandwe dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transf. to Entonjaneni dist., Apr., 1891; to Lower Umfolosi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z'land. pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apr., 1893; transf. as clk. and Zulu interp. to Nkandla dist., Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z'land pol., Apr., 1893; commanded detachment Z'land pol. with the special comm. annexation of Sambanans and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy commr., Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z'land., Dec., 1897; mag., Nkandla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904; mag., Umhlanga, 1905.
twin_text: FOXON, Cuthbert Colenso.—B. 1867; clk. and Zulu interp. to asst. consnr. and res. mag. Ndwandwe dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transf. to Entonjaneni dist., Apr., 1891; to Lower Umfolosi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z'land, pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apr., 1893; transf. as clk. and Zulu interp. to Nkandhla dist., Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z'land pol., Apr., 1893; commanded detachment Z'land pol. with the special consnr. annexation of Sambasas and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy consnr., Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z'land, Dec., 1897; mag., Nkandhla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904; mag., Umhlazi, 1905.

raw_text: FOXON, FRANK ERNEST.—3rd class clk. civ. serv., Natal, and 2nd clk. and Zulu interp., Umgendi div., Nov., 1890; ag. clk. of ct., registrar circuit ct., and sub-distributor of stamps, Weenen country, 1886; clk. of ct., dep. clk. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, UpperUmkomangi div., May, 1887; admstr. native law, Impendlele, Mar., 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. mag., Lower Tugela div., Apr. to June, 1890; ag. R.M. at Verulam and Stanger, 1890 to 1894; R.M. for col., and mag., Ndawedwe div., July, 1894; J.P. for col., 1893; served at commencement of Zulu war, 1879, in imp. transport dept., afterwards as lieut. Natal N. pioneers (medal and clasp); served as levy leader in command of Sibepu's contingent and Yamela's mounted men against Dinuzulu in Zululand, 1888; lieut., Natal carabiniers, Apr., 1889, capt., Dec., 1897; mag. Shortly after return to U.K. was asked to undertake reorganisation of Cape Colony postal service; acted as sec. and acctnt., P.O., Cape Colony, 1st Aug., 1880, to 31st July, 1881, when apptd. confirmed; introduced P.O. savings bk. system, 1st Jan., 1884; from which date acted as controller of branch in addition to other apptmts.; P.M.G. and gen. man. of telegraphs, Cape Colony and Basutoland, 27th Jan., 1892; also P.M.G., B. Bechuanaland, 1st Apr., 1893, until 1897, when that territory annexed to Cape Colony; P.M.G., Bechuanaland Prot., 1897; organised postal and telegraph services of Rhodesia, and acted as gen. man. of Rhodesian telegraph service, 1893-7, and man. Trans-Continental telegraph co., 1893-7; mem. of tender and advisory bd.; agt. gen. for Cape Colony in Lond., 1907.

Stlthms., 1892; chief just., Gibraltar, 1895; resig., Mar., 1906.

GOODRIDGE, HON. A. F.—Mem. of house of assem. for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, and 1882 to 1885; for Twillingate, 1885 to 1889; returned for same dist., 1893; mem. exec. coun., 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and again in 1894; after the resign. of the Whiteway ministry in Apr., 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry, and became premier of the col., which office he held till Dec., 1894; was a mem. of the bd. of wks. and rly. comsurs. under the Thorburn admnr. (1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chamb. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.

(1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chamb. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.

Glyn in the Transkei, 1887-8; dep. asst. adjt. and qrtmr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; asst. qrtmr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Suakin against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903; commd. 4th Army Corps, 1903-4; G.O.C. forces in Ireland, 1904.

Cunynghame; also staff offr. to Col. Glyn in the Transkei, 1887-8; dep. adjt. and qrtmr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; asst. qrtmr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Suakin against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903; comdtt. 4th Army Corps, 1903-4; G.O.C. forces in Ireland, 1904.

raw_text: GRINLINTON, Sir J. J., Kt. Bach. (1894), F.R.G.S., and A.I.C.E.—B. 1828; was employed for seven years on the Ordnance Survey of England; gazetted ensign in the 65th Regt.; apptd. depot adjt.; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855-6 as lieut. 4th King's Own Regt., and asst. engnr. during the siege of Sebastopol; was also engaged making a mil. survey of the allied positions; was mentioned in despatches on the fall of Sebastopol, 8th Sept., 1855; Crimean medal with clasp; 5th class of the Order of Medjidie, and the Turkish Medal; ast. survr-gen. of Ceylon, Sept., 1857; retd. from the army by the sale of his consn. Oct., 1858; apptd. an offl. councillor of the municipality of Colombo at its formation at 1866, and served for six years; resig. the public service, Oct., 1872, and subsequently followed mercantile pursuits; mem. of the central irrign. bd., July, 1887; a councillor of municipality of Colombo, 1887, and mem. legis. coun., Ceylon (to represent the gen. European community), Nov., 1888; comsrr. for Ceylon, Chicago Exhibtn., 1893.
twin_text: GRINLINTON, Sir J. J., Kt. BACH. (1894), F.R.G.S., and A.I.C.E.—B. 1828; was employed for seven years on the Ordnance Survey of England; gazetted ensign in the 65th Regt.; apptd. depot adjt.; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855-6 as lieut. 4th King's Own Regt., and asst. engrn. during the siege of Sebastopol; was also engaged making a mil. survey of the allied positions; was mentioned in despatches on the fall of Sebastopol, 8th Sept., 1855; Crimean medal with clasps; 5th class of the Order of Medjidie, and the Turkish Medal; asst. survr.-gen. of Ceylon, Sept., 1857; retd. from the army by the sale of his consm. Oct., 1858; appt. an offl. councillor of the municipality of Colombo at its formation at 1866, and served for six years; resig. the public service, Oct., 1872, and subsequently followed mercantile pursuits; mem. of the central irrign. bd., July, 1887; a councillor of municipality of Colombo, 1887, and mem. legis. coun., Ceylon (to represent the gen. European community), Nov., 1888; comsnr. for Ceylon, Chicago Exhibtn., 1893.

HALL, CAPT. W. DURHAM.—Asst. commr. of pol., G. Coast, 1894; served in Ashanti expd., 1895-6 (received the "Ashanti Star"); attached to London Med. Pol. for course of instr., and L.C.C. for instr. in weights and measures (received certif. from Council), 1896; ag. commr., of pol. sheriff, mag. and gov. of prs., G. Coast, 1898; ag. dist. commr., 1898; attached to 1st batt. Royal Sussex regt., 1898-99; passed school of instr., 1900; served with Ashanti expd., 1900 (specially selected to raise levies, ment. in despatch, medal and clasp); attached to 1st batt. Royal Rifle Reserve regt., 1900-01; offr.'s musketry certif., Hythe, 1901; apptd. to a company, W. African regt., May, 1901; ag. adjut., June, Dec., 1901; garrison adjut. and intell. offr. S. Leone, Jan., 1902, to May, 1903; A.D.C. to ag. govrs., S. Leone, 1902-3; asst. commr., N. Nigeria constab., 1903, and assisted in raising and organising the force; served in Bassa expd., N. Nigeria, 1904 (W. African general service medal and clasp); attached to Brighton pol. to course of instr., 1905; local commdt. of pol. and asst. commr., Cyprus, Dec., 1905.
twin_text: HALL, CAPT. W. DURHAM.—Asst. commr., pol., G. Coast, 1894; served in Ashanti exp., 1895-6 (received the "Ashanti Star"); attached to London Met. Coast, 1898; ag. dist. commr., 1898; attached to 1st batt. Royal Sussex regt., 1898-99; passed school of instr., 1900; served with Ashanti exp., 1900 (specially selected to raise levies, ment. in despatch medal and clasp); attached to 1st batt. Roy. Rifle Reserve regt., 1900-01; offr.'s musketeer certif., Hythe, 1901; apptd. to a company, W. African regt., May, 1901; ag. adjut., June, 1901; garrison adjut. and intell. offr., S. Leone, Jan., 1902, to May, 1903; A.D.C.; ag. govts., S. Leone, 1902-3; asst. commr., S. Nigeria constab., 1903, and assisted in raising and organising the force; served in Bassa exp., S. Nigeria, 1904 (W. African general service medal and clasp); attached to Brighton pol. for course of instr., 1905; local commdt. of pol. and asst. commr., Cyprus, Dec., 1905.

raw_text: HALLETT, WM. CARDY.—B. 1859; ed. at Mt. HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot batt. at Dum Dum in 1859; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volrs. in 1868 at their formation; asst. instr., G. Coast arm. nat. pol., 1875; dist. commr. of Accra, Oct., 1875; instr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Apr., 1877; asst. col. sec., G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius; adminstr. of the Gambia, 1885; adminstrd. the govt. of the W. Africa Sttlmts., from July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; gov., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yonnie expdn., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admstr., Barbados, 1891; gov., Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

raw_text: HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1870; ed. at King's Sch., Warwick; vol. clk. col. sec. offr., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1891; 3rd div. clk., dist. 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops in June and July, 1904; in command of depot, Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrena div. gov. prison and dep.coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as commr. Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Gov. lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. A.D.C. to high commr., Sir C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sec. s. 16th Feb., 1907; in cmd. of pol., Kyrena div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; asst. dist. astt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in command of Entebbe pol., and succ. cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907.

May, June and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrenia div., gov. of prison and dep.coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as comsnr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in med. Greek lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high comsnr., Sir C. A King-Harman and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sec.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in cmd. of pol., Kyrenia div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; asst. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in commd. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907.

twin_text: HODGSON, SIR FREDERIC MITCHELL, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1898), Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, (1903).—B. 1851; entered sav. bank dept. of Impl. P.O. and apptd., after compet. exam., Feb., 1869; was employed in the sec.'s office in connection with the transfer of the telegraphs to the state, 1868 to 1870, and apptd. clk. in that office, Aug., 1870; apptd., after further exam., to the 3rd class (grade 1) of the sec.'s office, Aug., 1876; promoted to 2nd class, Aug., 1880; was sen. capt. of the 24th Middlesex (post office), R.V.; postm.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1882; col. sec., G. Coast, Sept., 1888; adminstrd. the govt., June, 1889, to Feb., 1890; June to Nov., 1891; Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894; Apr. to Oct., 1896; and Dec., 1897, to March, 1898; major comdg. the G. Coast R.V., which he raised in 1892, 1892-1900; major comdg. Barbados volr. force, which he raised in 1901, 1901-1904; gov., G. Coast, Mar., 1898; gov. Barbados, Oct., 1900; gov. B. Guiana, 16th July, 1904.

raw_text: HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, F.R.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Italy, Felsted, and Mulgrave Castle; in Central Queensland, 1900 to 1902; joined Queensland contingent for S. Africa, 1902; apptd. to Transvaal and O.R.C. repat. dept., 1902; transf. to P.W.D., Transvaal, 1903; sub-inspr. Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; has twice served as asst. res. mag. while on duty in the Kalahari Desert on the German border; twice recd. spec. approval of S. of S. for service in connection with Lelotutu missions, 1905; mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; S.J.P., 1907.
twin_text: HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, F.R.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Italy, Felsted, and Mulgrave Castle; in Central Queensland, 1900 to 1902; joined Queensland contingent for S. Africa, 1902; apptd. to Transvaal and O.R.C. rept. dept., 1902; transfld. to P.W.D., Transvaal, 1903; sub.-inspr. Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; has twice served as asst. res. mag. while on duty in the Kalahari Desert on the German border; twice recd. spec. approval of S. of S. for service in connection with Lehotutu missions, 1905; mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; S.J.P., 1907.

raw_text: HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK.—Formerly mem. for Stellenbosch in Cape house of assembly; mem. of exec. coun., 1881; deleg. to col. confce., 1887 and 1894.
twin_text: HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK.—Formerly mem. for Stellenbosch in Cape house of assembly; mem. of exec. coun., 1881; deleg. to col. confee., 1887 and 1894.

raw_text: HOGUE, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1846; M.L.A. for The Glebe, New S. Wales since 1894; min. of pub. instr. and min. for labour, 1889-9; col. sec., 1904-7; min. of pub. instrn. and min. for labour since May, 1907.
twin_text: HOGUE, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1846; M.L.A. for The Glebe, New S. Wales since 1894; min. of pub. instr. and min. for labour, 1898-9; col. sec., 1904-7; min. of pub. instrn. and min. for labour since May, 1907.

raw_text: HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stlttns., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908.
twin_text: HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stltmts., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908.

raw_text: HOLDER, THE HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1850; mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1887; treasr., 1889 to Aug., 1890; premier and treasr., June to Oct., 1892; comsrr. of public works, June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; treasr., Apr., 1894, to Dec., 1899; premier and treasr., Dec., 1899, to May, 1901; mem. hse. of represent., Austnl. parl., 1901; speaker, 1901.
twin_text: HOLDER, THE HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1850; mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1887; treasr. 1889 to Aug., 1890; premier and treasr., June to Oct., 1892; comsnr. of public works, June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; treasr., Apr., 1894, to Dec., 1899; premier and treasr., Dec., 1899, to May, 1901; mem. hse. of represent., Austrln. parlty., 1901; speaker, 1901.

raw_text: HOLLAND, BERNARD H., C.B. (1904).—B. 1856; ed. at Eton and Triin. Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1882; in office of charity comsrr., 1884-1890; priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1892-1894; sec. to royal comsrr. on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, 1894-1896; to Ld. Rothschild's comtees on old-age pensions, 1896-1898; to Transvaal concessions comsrr., 1900-1901; to royal comsrr. on Port of London, 1901-2; and to royal comsrr. on war in S. Africa, 1902-3; priv. sec. to Right Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1903; ditto to Lord Elgin, 1905-1908; author of "Imperium et Libertas," etc.
twin_text: HOLLAND, BERNARD H., C.B. (1904).—B. 1836; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1882; in office of charity comm., 1884-1890; priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1892-1894; sec. to royal comm. on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, 1894-1896; to Ld. Rothschild's comte, on old-age pensions, 1896-1898; to Transvaal concessions comsn., 1900-1901; to royal comm. on Port of London, 1901-2; and to royal comm. on war in S. Africa, 1902-3; priv. sec. to Right Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1903; ditto to Lord Elgin, 1905-1908; author of "Imperium et Libertas," etc.

raw_text: HOLLAND, J.—B. 1858; apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 18th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 1904.
twin_text: HOLLAND, J.—B. 1858; apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 18th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 194.

raw_text: HOLLINGSWORTH, A. H.—A.M. Inst. C.E.; ed. King's Coll., London, engineering dept.; art. pupil, borough and water engr., Croydon; asst. engr., P.W.D., Hong-Kong, Dec., 1896; ag. exec. engr., in charge of water and drain. wks., Aug., 1900; ag. consulting sanitary survr., Sept., 1900; exec. engr. in charge of water and drain. wks., 1st Jan., 1902.
twin_text: HOLLINGSWORTH, A.H.—A.M. Inst. C.E.—ed. King's Coll., London, engineering dept.; art. pupil, borough and water engr., Croydon; asst. borough and water engr., Croydon; asst. engr., P.W.D., Hong-Kong, Dec., 1896; ag. exec. engr. in charge of water and drain. wks., Aug., 1900; ag. consulting sanitary survr., Sept., 1900; exec. engr. in charge of water and drain. wks., 1st Jan., 1902.

raw_text: HOLLIS, ALFRED CLAUD.—Ed. Highgate and St. Leonards, and in Switzerland and Germany; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 12th Mar., 1897; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admnstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. consmr. from Feb., 1901, to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admnstr., 1st Apr., 1903; reg.-gen. of marriages, 1st Dec., 1902; reg.-gen. of births and deaths, 1st Oct., 1904; sec. for native affairs, 13th June, 1907; African general serv. medal, Jubaland clasp.
twin_text: HOLLIS, ALFRED CLAUD.—Ed. Highgate and St. Leonards, and in Switzerland and Germany; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 12th Mar., 1897; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admtn. and priv. sec. to H.M. consmr. from Feb., 1901, to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admtn., 1st Apr., 1903; reg.-gen. of marriages, 1st Dec., 1902; reg.-gen. of births and deaths, 1st Oct., 1904; sec. for native affairs, 13th June, 1907; African general serv. medal, Jubaland clasp.

raw_text: HOLME, HENRY FREDERICK.—4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Leeward Islands, 1898; ag. 3rd clk., 1898 and 1900; ag. priv. sec., 1900 and 1901; ag. 2nd clk., 1900 and 1905; 2nd clk., 1901; supt., printing office, 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., 1906, 1907, and 1908; clk., legis. coun., Antigua, 1908.
twin_text: HOLME, HENRY FREDERICK.—4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Leeward Islands, 1898; ag. 3rd clk., 1898 and 1900; ag. priv. sec., 1900 and 1901; ag. 2nd clk., 1900 and 1905; 2nd clk., 1901; supt., printing office, 1905; ag. ast. col. sec., 1906, 1907, and 1908; clk., legis. coun., Antigua, 1908.

raw_text: HOLMES, JOHN RICHARD.—S.S.C., 1879; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1889; dist. consmr., G. Coast, 1889; commanded detachment in Awoonah expdn., 1889-91; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1899.
twin_text: HOLMES, JOHN RICHARD.—S.S.C., 1879; bar-rister, Mid. Tem., 1889; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1889; commanded detachment in Awoonah expdn., 1889-91; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1889.

raw_text: HOLROYD, SIR EDWARD DUNIAS, KT. BACH. (1903).—B. 1828; ed. at Winchester (Queen's gold medal for Latin and English essays), Trin. Coll. Camb. (B.A. 1st cls. tripus, 1851, M.A., 1854), student, Gray's Inn, 1851; called to the bar, 1855; called to Victorian bar, 1859; Tasmanian bar, 1867; Q.C., 1879; raised to bench, Aug., 1881; ex-pres. of Impl. Fed. League of Victoria; sen. puisne judge of sup. ct. of Victoria; ret. 1st Aug., 1906.
twin_text: HOLROYD, SIR EDWARD DUNDAS, KT. BACH. (1903).—B. 1828; ed. at Winchester (Queen's gold medal for Latin and English essays), Trin. Coll. Camb. (B.A. 1st cls. tripus, 1851, M.A., 1854); student, Gray's Inn, 1851; called to the bar, 1859; called to Victorian bar, 1859; Tasmanian bar, 1867; Q.C., 1879; raised to bench, Aug., 1881; ex-pres. of Impl. Fed. League of Victoria; sen. puisne judge of sup. ct. of Victoria; ret. 1st Aug., 1906.

raw_text: HOLTZEB, MAURICE WM., F.I.S., etc.—Govt. gardener, Port Darwin, 1878; botanic gardener, 1889; dir. botanic garden, Adelaide, 1891.

HOMAGEE, JAMES FRANCIS, I.S.O. (1906).—Court messenger, St. Helena, 1859; clk. o ch. just., 1862; man. govt.savings bk., 1865; clk. of the peace, taxing master, and registr. sup. ct., 1847; legal adviser, crown prosecutor, and registr. V.-A. ct., 1870; also supervisor of customs and registr. of shipping, in 1883; admity. advoc., 1890.
twin_text: HOMAGEE, JAMES FRANCIS, L.S.O. (1906).—Court messenger, St. Helena, 1859; clk. o.c.h. just., 1862; man. govt. savings bk., 1865; clk. of the peace, taxing master, and regist. sup., 1867; legal adviser, crown prosecutor, and regist. V.-A. ct., 1870; also supervisor of customs and regist. of shipping, in 1883; admtny. advoc., 1890.

raw_text: HOMBURGI, ROBERT.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., since 1884; atty.-gen., 1890-92 and 1892-3; ditto and min. of educn., 1904-5; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1905.

HONEY, JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1908).—Served under Cape govt., 1880 to 1901; transf. to Transvaal when collr. and prin. controller of cust. and prin. registr. of shipping; served in Gaika-Galeka and Basutoland campaigns, holding comsrr. in latter as lieut. Capetown Rifles; dir. of cust., Transvaal, and cust. adviser to high comsrr., 16th May, 1901; M.L.C., Mem. I.C.C.; chrmn., tender and post office investment boards.
twin_text: HONEY, JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1908).—Served under Cape govt., 1880 to 1901; transf'd. to Transvaal when collr. and prin. controller of cust. and prin. regist. of shipping; served in Gaika-Galeka and Basutoland campaigns, holding comsn. in latter as lieut. Capetown Rifles; dir. of cust., Transvaal, and cust. adviser to high comsnr., 16th May, 1901; M.L.C., Mem. I.C.C.; chrmn., tender and post office investment boards.

raw_text: HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE.—Served in B.S.A. Co.'s expdt., to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B. C. Africa admnstr., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 9th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acting offr., 20th Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland adminstr., 22nd Mar., 1907. "Cartier," and "Les États-Unis, origines, constitution et développement."
twin_text: HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE.—Served in B.S.A. Co.'s expdt., to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B. C. Africa admtn., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 9th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acting offr., 26th Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admtn., 22nd Mar., 1907.

raw_text: DE GRITCHY, A. NICOLLE M.B.C.M., Edin.—Ast. med. offr., and J.P., Victoria, Seychelles, and outlying islands, 11th Sept., 1901; col. surg., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1906.
DE KRETSER, EDWARD, I.S.O. (1903)—B. 1854; employed in col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, 8th May, 1872; astl. and gen., 1st Jan., 1901; astl. commr. of rev., Mar., 1907.

DE SARAM, J.H., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1844; writer to the govt. of Ceylon, 1865; pol. mag. of Balapiti-medura, 1866; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; commr. of requests, Colombo, 1868; dist. judge, Kurunegala, 1875; regis.-gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; regis.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto, Kandy, Aug., 1893; ret.
DEWDNAYE, THE HON. EDGAR.—Lieut.-gov. of N.W. territories, 1881; min. of interior, Canada, 1888; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, 1892-7.

DOWER, EDWARD EBENEZER.—Cik., ch. mag.'s off., Kokstad, C. Colony, Oct., 1891; Matatiele, Jan., 1896; nat. affairs offr., Aug., 1896; prin. elk., 1900; ch. elk., July, 1901; also acting offr., Prime Minister's dept., July, 1904; asst. sec. to nat. affairs dept., July, 1906; sec. to ditto, Sept., 1907.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, school, Ball Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; priv. CLK., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906.
ELLIOT, MAJOR SIR HENRY GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1826; ch. mag. of Tembuland, C. of Good Hope; in command of Tembu levies during Basuto war; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES BLETTERMAN, LL.B., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1841; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1859; clk. to Justice Watermeyer, 1863; sec. to examrs. in literature and science; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1867; ch. clk. dept. of lands and wks., Dec., 1872; advoc. sup., 1875; asst. commnr., crown lands and pub. wks., 1876; gen. manager, rly.s., Dec., 1880; is mem. of coun. of Univ., Cape; examr. and moderator of examrs. in science; ret., 1904.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1850; apptd., 29th Dec., 1869, after passing an exam. before the civ. ser. comsrs., clk.in the acct. branch in the office of the sec. of state for the col., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1868; 1st class asst. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; bookkeeper and sen. astt. to the financial clk., 1884; acted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1896.

ENNISS, GEORGE FRANCIS MACHANIEL.—B. 1868; ed. at King's Coll., school and privately; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1892; sec. to gov., B.N. Borneo, 13th Nov., 1894; sessions judge, B.N. Borneo and Labuan, 24th Dec., 1894; judge of genl. ct., Labuan, 1895; judge of ch. ct., B.N. Borneo, 14th Aug., 1895; ag. res., Province Alcock, B.N. Borneo, 13th Dec., 1895; resign., Oct., 1897; ag. astt. judge, Zanzibar, 18th May to 7th July, and 1st Aug. to 24th Oct., 1899; regis., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1899; town mag., Mombasa, 20th July, 1900; ag. judge, E. Africa Prot., 6th Sept., 1900; ag. astt. judge, Zanzibar, 25th Jan., 1901; legal vice-consul, Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; judge of high ct. of Uganda, 11th Aug., 1902, and judge of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; joint author of "The Registration of Transfers."
EVERY, The Rev. Edward Francis, M.A.—Bishop of the Falklands, 1902.

FARFAN, Joseph Francis Ambrose.—Ed. at Ushaw Coll., Durham; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised at Trinidad bar from 1877 to 1904; mem. of comm. to inquire into difficulties existing between agricultural contractors and proprietors, and in collaboration with late Mr. Justice Lewis drafted "Agricultural Contracts Orde," 1889; retained on several occasions by the Crown to prosecute in criminal cases; mag., Arima, Trinidad, 16th Nov., 1904; ag. S.J.P. of Port-of-Spain on several occasions, 1904 to 1908; chmn. of comm. to inquire into frauds of Diego Martin Local Road Bd., 1906.
FFRENCH-MULLEN, VINCENT, L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. med. offr., Port Maria, Jamaica, Apr., 1881.

FIDDES, GEORGE VANDELEUR, C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll. and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd. after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acctnt. to Sir A. Milner, high comsnr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907.

FORESTER-WALKER, Gen. Sir Frederic William Edward Forester, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1878), C.M.G. (1886). Knight of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1844; ed. at Sandhurst; entd. Scots. Guards, 1862; capt. and lieut.-gen., 1873; col., 1878; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1902; served on staff in Kafir war, 1877-8; mil. sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, 1878-9; in Zulu war, 1879 (ment. in desps.); A.A. and Q.M.G., home district during war in Egypt, 1882; A.A. and Q.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1884; commdd. 2nd brig. at Aldershot, 1889-90; commdd. troops in Egypt, 1890-95; western dist., 1895-99; and troops in S. Africa, 1899; commdd. lines of communication in S. African war, 1899-1901; and troops in Cape Colony, 1900-1; gov. and commd-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1st Aug., 1906.

FOSBERY, Widenham Francis Widenham, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1869; cons. agt., Niger Coop. Prot., 1893; ag. vice-coms., 1894-5; dist. comsrs., 1896; ag. div. comsrs., 1897-8; pol. offr., centra' division expdt., 1898 (severely wounded); res., Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Benin Terr. expdt., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comsrs., 1900; pol. offr., Ishan expdt., 1901 (medal with clasp).

FREEER, GERALD DUDLEY, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1888, M.B. (Lond.), 1898, D.P.H. (Lond.), 1904—B. 1866; House surg. gen. hosp., Birmingham; house surg. gen. hosp., Singapore, July, 1890; col. surg. Malacca, Feb., 1893; ag. col. surg. res. Singapore, Oct., 1896; col. surg.-res., Penang, 1897; ag. col. surg., Penang, June, 1900; principal, med. schl., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1905; mem. S.S. med. coun., July, 1905.
FULLARTON, R.—Harbourmr., Melbourne, 1877; ch. harbourmr., pres. pilot bd., and chmn. steam navig. bd., 1882; capt. comding. Victorian naval brigade, 1871; mem. of coun. of defence, 1884.

GEBERS, H. L.—Student Int. S.N.A. Dep., 1894; clk., Zulu and D. int. mag. ct., Dundee, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1895; 2nd cls. clk., 1900; lent to Transvaal, 1901; clk. of ct. P. P. Vryheid, 1901; J.P., Vryheid, 1901; A. A. M., Vryheid, 1902; reverted to Natal service, 1903; transf'd to mag. ct., Dundee, 1904, senior clk. and A. A. M.
GEBERS, WILHELM FRIEDRICH.—Asst. inspr. of native educ., Natal, 1st Mar., 1903; inspr., ditto, 1st July, 1904; sub-inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1906.

GILL, THOMAS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered col. service, S. Australia, Feb., 1865; accmnt.; treasy., Jan., 1883; under treasy., 1894; also registrar of inscribed stock in Adelaide, 1895; mem. of supply and tender bd., 1903; and mem. of mun. tramways trust, Jan., 1907.
GILL, WALTER, F.L.S., F.R.H.S.—Conservator of forests, S. Australia, July, 1890.
GILLIGAN, GEOFFREY G.—B. 1876; capt., 4th Battn. A. and S. Highrs.; served in S. Africa, 1900-01 (medal with 3 clasps); inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, 1904; asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Jan., 1906.

GILLOTT, HON. SIR SAMUEL, KT. BACH. (1901).—B. 1838; M.L.A. for East Melbourne, Victoria, since Feb., 1899; min. without portfolio, 1900-01; atty.-gen., 1901-02; Mayor of Melbourne, 1900-02; first Lord Mayor, 1902-3; chief sec. and min. of labour, Victoria, Feb., 1904; has now retired.

GILMAN, EDWARD WILMOT FRANCIS.—B. 1876; ed. at Bradford and Brasenose Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1898; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Nov., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, June, 1902; ag. asst. supt. of Indian immigrants, Penang, Apr., 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; sent on spec. miss. to India in connection with Indian immigration, Sept., 1903; emigr. agt. in Madras for the S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1907.

GILSON, CAPT. CHAS. HUGH.—D.S.O.; served in B.S.A. police and Natal civ. ser.; served in Matabele rebellion, 1896-7; dist. comdt., S.A.C., Swaziland, 1902 to 1907; asst. consmr., comdg. Swaziland police, 23rd Mar., 1907.
GIROUARD, DESIRE, K.C.—Ed. Montreal Coll.; called to the bar, Canada, 1869; K.C. 1876; represented Jacques Cartier co., 1878 to 1895; judge sup. ct., Canada, 1895.

GIROUARD, LT.-COL. SIR EDWARD PERCY CRANWILL, K.C.M.G. (1900), D.S.O., R.E.—Served with Dongola expedit. force, 1896 (desp., brevet-major, British medal, Khedive's medal, 2 clasps); Nile exped., 1897 (desp., clasp); rlyw. traf. man., Woolwich Arsenal, 1890-5; dir. of Soudan rlyws., 1890-8; pres. Egyptian rlyw. bd., 1898-9 (2nd cls. Medjidie); S. African war, 1899-1902 (desp.); consmr. of rlyws., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902; mem. I.C.C.; resign. 1904; high consmr., N. Nigeria, 12th Feb., 1907; gov., ditto, 18th Apr., 1908.

GLASGOW, 7TH EARL OF, THE RIGHT HON. SIR DAVID BOYLE, G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1833; capt. (retired) R.N.; served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857 (medal and clasp); J.P. for Ayrshire, and chmn. of co. coun.; gov. of N.Z., 1892-97.

GLASIER, FRANK BEDFORD.—B. 1872; asst. civ. engrnr., Bermuda harbr.wks., June, 1894; engrnr.-in-charge, Coatracoalcos harbr. wks., Mexico, May, 1896; dist. engrnr., S. Leone govt. rly., June, 1897; dist. engrnr., Tarkwa rly., Gold Coast, Oct., 1898; and gen. manager, S. Leone govt. rly., May, 1899; promoted gen. manager, Lagos govt. rly., Apr., 1901.

GLOVER, H. T.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and temporarily employed by the roy. comm. on the poor laws, May, 1906; to Feb., 1907; in the B. of T., Feb. to Aug., 1907; at the civ. ser. comm., Sept. to Nov., 1907; assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907.

GLYDE, ADOLPHUS YEOVL.—Apptd. clk. in land titles dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1882; dep. regsr. of titles, ag. regsr. of deeds, sub. collr. of internal rev., asst. regsr. of titles, 1893; ag. regsr. of deeds, Nov., 1896, to Jan., 1897.
GOBEIL, ANTOINE, I.S.O. (1904).—Law clk., dept. of pub. wks., Canada, 1879 to 1885; then sec., dep. min.of pub. wks., Jan., 1891.

GOODMAN SIR WILLIAM MEIGH, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Univ. Coll.; graduated B.A. (honours) at the Univ. of Lond., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1870; went S.E. circ. and Surrey sessions; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1883; ch. just., 1886; comsnr. to revise and consolidate the laws of the col., 1886; atty.-gen. Hong Kong, 1889; ag. col. sec., May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1896, Jan. to May, 1896, and Apr.-1899, to Feb., 1900; K.C., 1900; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1st April, 1902; ret., 1905.

GRABHAM, Michael, M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lon., M.A., M.B., B.C., Camb., 1891.—B. 1866; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; St Thomas' Hosp., Lon.; B.A., Camb., 1887; 1st cl. honours nat. sciences, tripes and exhibitioner, St. John's Coll.; asst. med. offr., city asylum, Birmingham, 3rd Nov., 1891; surg. to the lying-in hospital and general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 1st Apr., 1892; re-apptd., 1st July, 1899; served on cattle diseases comsn., 1894; late sec. med. council of Jamaica, 1897; mem. bd. of governors Institute of Jamaica, 1902; joint author, with F. V. Theobald, S.E. Agric. Coll., "A Monograph on the Mosquitoes of Jamaica," 1905; author of various papers on same subject, 1906 and 1907.
GREENE, Geoffrey Philip.—B. 1868; gen. mag., Ceylon rlyws., 11th Oct., 1901.

GROOM, Hon. LITTLETON ERNEST, M.A., LL.M.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elected to 1st hse. of rep., C. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of royal comsrs. on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of dist. ct., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., 5th July, 1903; atty-gen., 13th Oct., 1906.

HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1850; ed. at Westminster schl., and at Chr. Ch., Oxford; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-gov. of Natal, Mar., 1877; ag. clk., col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1880; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1881; acted as col. treas. and also as col. sec., 1885; col. sec., 1887; was chmn. of Natal trade comm., 1885-6; deleg. to customs and rly. confere., Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov., Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; ret., 1893, on introduction of responsible gov.
HAGGART, THE HON. JOHN G.—Postmtr.-gen., Canada, 1888; min. of rlys. and canals, 1892-6.

HAHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschool, Hamburg; apprenticeship under J. A. Biernatzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the technological instn., Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1887; foreman, pubs., Jan., 1888; ag. asst. engr., N. Div. in 1889, 1890-1; asst. engr., N. Div., 1891; asst. engr. of works and buildings, Feb. 1894; asst. engr. office and wks., Nov., 1899.

HAINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor and chaplain, Selangor, 1890; inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1891; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1899; also ag. headmnr., High schl., Malacca, 1899-1900; col. chap., Penang, Apr., 1901; (Surrogate), hon. chaplain, Penang volunteer corps.

HALCOMB, FRED., M.A. (Wadham Coll., Oxon).—B. 1836; parly. librarian, S. Australia, Jan., 1870; clk., asst. and serg.-at-arms, legis. coun., Apr., 1874; clk. house of assem., May, 1887; clk., legis. coun., and clk. of parliaments, 1901.

HALE, ABRAHAM.—Ag. mag., Matang, 13th Dec., 1884; insp. of mines and asst. mag., Kinta, Feb., 1885; collector and mag., Tampin, 17th Oct., 1887; ag. sup. of Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1888; clk. of coun., 24th Oct., 1889; asst. Indian immigr. agent, Dec., 1889; ag. for resident, Feb. to Mar., 1900; offr. in charge, Mar. to Nov., 1894; dist. offr., Tampin 1st Jan., 1896; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, 26th Jan., 1897; passed higher standard in Malay, Apr., 1897; examiner in Malay, 1898; dist. offr., Klang, 9th Mar., 1899; collector of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regisr. of titles, Selangor; chmn., pub. gardens comtee., Kuala Lumpur, and of museum comtee., 21st Nov., 1899; vice-chmn., Malay agric. attnt., 23rd Sept., 1900; dir. of W. and O. fund, 19th Apr., 1901; dist. offr., U. Selangor, Jan., 1903; ditto, Larut and Krian, and regisr. of titles, Perak North, Mar., 1904.

HALL, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1848; volr. col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, Nov., 1866; clk., gen. bd. of health, June, 1867; clk. med. dept., Nov., 1868; acct., immigr. dept., June, 1872; senior clk. Oct., 1880; and ag. ch. clk. several times from 1880 to 1886; inspr. of immigr., 1886; ag. storekr.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1898; do., Sept., 1901; ditto, 12th Aug., 1903, to 19th Aug., 1904; confirmed storekr.-gen., 1st June, 1907.

HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Ed. at Harrow schl.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1891; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1891; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; supt. of educn., Penang, Oct., 1894; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Nov., 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1897; sheriff and dep. regisr., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1897; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, Mar., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as collr. ld. rev., Penang, till Mar., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Sept., 1900, and confirmed, Aug., 1902; sen. dist. offr., July, 1904; ag. P.M.G., Aug., 19-4; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1905; ag. aud.-gen., S. Settlmts., Aug. to Dec., 1907.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; and to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf'd to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.

HAUFREY, WILFRED R. J.—Lieut. 2nd batt. Royal Fusiliers; asst. supt. of pol., S. Stiltnitz, Oct., 1901; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Dec., 1906, to Feb., 1907; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Apr., 1908.
HEMSTED, CHARLES SELWOOD.—3rd treasy. asst., E. Africa Prot., 20th Apr., 1900; asst. collr., 1st Apr., 1903; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1907.

HEMSTED, RUPERT WILLIAM.—2nd treasy. asst., E. Africa Prot., 19th Sept., 1899; ch. acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901, to 1st Dec., 1903; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1904; collr., 17th Dec., 1906.
HOBBS, STEPHEN BARTON.—Lower mechanical clk., survr-gen's. dept., Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1866; gov't. survr., 25th Apr., 1892; on spec. duty at Seychelles, 1897-1900.
HOLTZE, MAURICE WM., F.L.S., etc.—Govt. gardener, Port Darwin, 1878; botanic gardener, 1889; dir. botanic garden, Adelaide, 1891.
HOMBURG, ROBERT.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., since 1884; atty.-gen., 1890-92 and 1892-3; ditto and min. of educn., 1904-5; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1905.
HOOD, Hon. Joseph Henry.—B. 1846; M.A.; puisne judge, Victoria, since 1890.

HOOD, Samuel James.—B. 1868; asst. warehouse keeper, Br. Hond., Oct., 1878; 4th clk., treas. and cust., Mar., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; clk., reg.-gen., June, 1893; superv. of cust., Gold Coast, Feb., 1894; insp. superv., Nov., 1893; asst. col. of cust., S. Leone, Jan., 1901; ag. collr., Feb. to Aug., 1901; asst. comp. of cust., Gold Coast, Oct., 1901; ag. compt., Feb. to June, and Aug. to Dec., 1902, and Aug., 1903, to Feb., 1904; collr. of cust., Lagos, June, 1904; ag. col. treas., Lagos, Oct., 1905, to Apr., 1906; provl. collr. of cust., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; comptroller of cust., G. Coast, July, 1908.
