Guiana, 1885; 5th ast. survr. clk., regist'r. of off., 1890; passed exam. as sworn clk. and notary pub., 1893, and acted as such on various occasions; charge of Berbice off., Aug. to Dec., 1896; asst. clk., 1896; clk. to atty.-gen., 1898; special comte of legislature on Demerara Essequibo rly. tariff, 1899.

RANKINE, RICHARD SIMS DONKIN.—B. 1857; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to George O'Brien, Sept., 1897, to 1901; acted as ex. coun. Sept., 1899, to Mar., 1900; apptd. native taxes, treasury, 1901; priv. sec. to Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to E. im Thurn, 10th Oct., 1904; conf. sec., Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific, clk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific comsn., June, 1905, and from Nov., 1907, to Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and asst. recvr.-gen., Jan., 1909.

RANNIE, CLARENCE RUPERT.—B. 1875; clerical assist., audit dept., Antigua, May, 1891; clk. to col. engtn., Antigua, Mar., 1891; ag. to audr.-gen., Sept., 1894; 2nd clk. to audr.-gen., Leeward Is., Jan., 1895; audit clk., Dominica, Mar., 1897, to Aug., 1900; cashier, treas., Sept., 1900; 2nd indoor offr., treas., 14th Nov., 1904; ag. 1st indoor offr., 23rd Nov., 1904, to 20th Dec., 1905.

RASON, HON. CORNHWAITHE HERBERT B. 1859; ed. at Brighton, Eastbourne & Reading; M.L.C. for Swan, W. Aust., 1887; M.L.A. for S. Murchison, 1897-1901; direc. Guildford since 1901; min. of works, 1901; min. of works and rlyws., 1902-4; prem. of W. Ast. 1905; pres. of royal comm. on mining, 1898, and of royal comm. on immigr., 1905; agt.-gen. in London for W. Aust., 1906.

RAT, Joseph Numa.—B. 1848; colonial scholarship, Trinidad, 1866; Warneford scholarship, King's Coll., Lond., 1867; seven times prize winner, literature, divinity and medicine in King's Coll., 1867-1871; matric., Lond. Univ., 1868; prel. science, M.B. Lond. Univ., 1869; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1873; asst. col. surg., G. Coast, Feb., 1887; dist. med. offr., Dominica, Aug., 1889; ag. med. offr., public institutions, Dominica, 1890; dist. med. offr., St. Kitts, Oct., 1892; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Dec., 1897; dep. judge, court of summary juris., Leeward Is., 1899; dist. med. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1905; M.L.C., Dominica, 1888-1889; chmn., town improvement commsrs., St. Kitts, 1896-1897; mem. poop law bd., St. Kitts, 1896-1897; has translated several military drill books into the Hausa language for the G. Coast gov't.; has also written numerous papers on Leprosy, Yaws, etc., grammars of the Carib and Hausa languages, and a translation of the Gospel of St. Mark into the French patois of the W. Indies.

RATTRAY, W. Wallace.—3rd clk., gov.'s office, Windward Is., 1883; translt. to Grenada as 2nd clk. in gov.'s office on separation of Barbados from Windward Is., June, 1885; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. controller of customs, May to Sept., 1892, and Feb. to May, 1894; asst. treas., G. Coast, Apr., 1895; ag. treas., May to June, 1898, and Dec. to Mar., 1900 with a seat in the exec. and legis. councils; sent to Gambia on spec. serv., April, 1901, as ag. treas., collr. of cust. and postmtr., J.P., and commsr. ct. of requests, and commsr. of affidav.; mem. exec. and legis. comms.; asst. coll. of customs, Lagos, 1902; ag. collr. of cust., 1902; asst. treas., May, 1904; ag. treas., May to Sept., 1904 asst. compt. of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904.

RAWSON, ADMIRAL Sir Harry Holdsworth, G.C.B.(1906); K.C.B.(1897); C.B.(1882).—B. 1843; naval cadet, 1857; sub-lieut., 1863; lieut., 1863; commander, 1871; captain, 1877; rear-admiral, 1892; vice-admiral, 1898; admiral, 1903; hoisted the British flag at Nicosia, Cyprus, 1878, and was for one month mil. comdr. of that place; principal transport offr. in Egypt, 1882 (medal, star 3rd cls., Osmanisch, C.B.); A.D.C. to H.M. Queen Victoria, 1890 to 1892; in cmd. of expdn. against M'Barack and capture of Mwale, 1895 (medal); of bombardment of Sultan's palace, Zanzibar, 1896 (1st cls. Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, and promoted to Order of Hamoudieh), and of Benin, 1897 (clasp and K.C.B.); apptd. command-in-chief, Cape of Good Hope and W. Africa Station, 1895; in command of Channel Squadron, 1898-1901; gov. of N. S. Wales, 12th Feb., 1902.

RAWSTORNE, Henry Clancy.—Asst. offr., civil status, Mauritius, Jan., 1874; clk., stip. et. Flaq., Mar., 1875; asst. clk., dist. ct., Port Louis, July, 1875; joint clk., ditto, Oct., 1876; inspr. of pol. force, May, 1877; inspr. inland rev., Mar., 1880; and supt. of distilleries, Mar., 1883; ag. supt. inland rev., May to Oct., 1885; supt. of distilleries and inland rev., 1st July, 1905.

RAY, GEORGE CECIL, LINGARD.—Entd. Br. Cent. Africa adminstr., June, 1893; apptd. asst. collr. in W. Shire dist.; led punitive expdn. agst. Chief Malolo, 1895; judicial offr., 1897; collr. of Manji dist., 1897-1901, during which period acted as political offr. accompanying Shirwa expln., and recd. B.C.A. medal and clasp; also led punitive expdn. agst. Chief Namwada; collr. and vice-consul, S. Nyassa dist., 1901; 1st cls. asst., 1902; ag. vice-consul at Chinde, E. Africa, Apr. to Oct., 1903; returned to S. Nyassa dist. as collr., vice-consul and dist. mag.; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, May to Nov., 1906.

RAYNER, Sir Thomas Crossley, K.T. (1899).—K.C., Br. Guiana (1903).—B. 1860; ed. at Owens Coll.; matric., Lond., 1878; barr., Mid. Tem., 1882; dist. commsr., Gold Coast Col., 1887; acted as judge, 1890; mag., Trinidad, 1890; ag. puisne judge, June to Oct., 1891; chmn. of comm. of inquiry into registry of sup. ct., Jan., 1892; ag. commsr., Tobago, Sept. to Dec., 1892; ag. puisne judge, Trinidad, Dec., 1892 to Sept., 1893; puisne judge, G. Coast, Jan., 1894; ch. just., Lagos, 1895; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1902; chancrl. of diocese of Guiana, 1903; compiled new edition of laws of Br. Guiana, 1904-5.

READ, EDWARD INSKIP, F.R.C.S.I.—Entered govt. service, Trinidad, Feb., 1882; ag. dist. med. offr., S. Naparima and town, and Port of San Fernando, 1882-3; asst. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1884; surg.-in-charge, San Fernando hosp., 1887; ag. health offr. of shipping, inspr. of immigr., and dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, 1891; in charge of coll. hosp., Port of Spain, Nov., 1892; clinical asst., Royal Westminster Ophthal. Hosp., 1893; dist. med. offr., Santa Cruz; examiner to widows' and orphans' civ. ser. fund, 1897; ophthalmic surg. to health office dept., 1900.

READ, Herbert James, C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; 1st class clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., 1st Apr., 1905; British delegate at African liquor conf., Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundy. comm. at Berlin for the setttn. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor conf., at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comte. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908.

READ, THE VEN. ARCHDEACON J. H., D.D.—1st Archdeacon of P. E. Is., 1860; ecclesiast. commsr., 1888; formerly of St. John's Coll., Camb., and B.D., 1852; D.D., 1857, of King's Coll., Fredericton, N.B.; rector of Milton, 1865.
READ, WILLIAM HENRY MACLEOD, C.M.G. (1886).—M.L.C., S. Stiths., 1867-85.

REAL, Hon. PATRICK.—B. 1847; called to the bar, Queensland, 1874; frequently acted as dist. ct. judge, and for some years was crown proscr. in cent. dist.; mem. of royal comm. on establishment of a Queensland Univ., 1891; judge of sup. ct., Queensland, 1890.
REDDIE, Colin Spittal.—Imperial Brit. East Africa Co.; collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1895.

REECE, EARLIE BRANWELL.—B. 1876; ent. pub. service, St. Lucia, Apr., 1892, as superv. mry. clk. govt. off.; 3rd clk., Apr., 1893; 2nd clk. and accnt., treas., Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk. on six occasions, 1895-9; honours graduate, 1900 (diploma), Ont. business coll., Canada (affil. inst. ch., accnt.), Canada; apptd. chief clk. and book keeper, treasury, Gold Coast, 26th April, 1901; introduced double entry system of accounts in the treasury in conformity with fin. inst., June, 1901; acctnt., Jan., 1902; sen. asst. treas., July, 1905; ag. chief acctnt., G. Coast govt. rlyw., Feb., 1907; chief acctnt., 1st June, 1907.
REECE, FREDERICK ST. AUBYN.—Ck., P.O., Barbados, 1873; 2nd ck., 1875; resig. 1878; landing waiter, St. Lucia, 1883; col. postmtr., St. Lucia, 1885.

REECE, REV. JAS. EBENZER.—B. 1847; ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. in 1868; M.A. in 1874; ag. curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, Barbados, and priv. sec. to Bishop Parry, 1872; ast. master of Harrison Coll., and curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1877; curate of St. Bartholomew's, 1878; curate of St. Luke's, 1882; bishop's chaplain, 1883; inspr. of schls., 1885; canon of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1905.

REECE, I. RICHARD.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. in 1870; called to bar, Inn. Tem., 1871; J.P., Barbados, 1873; coroner for parishes of Christchurch and St. George, 1873; ag. provost-marshal and serjt.-at-arms, 1874; ag. pol. mag. of St. Michael's parish, 1875; ag. judge of ast. ct. of appeal, 1875-79; comsrr. of probates, 1877; ag. solr.-gen., 1880; registr. of friendly and building societies, 1880; ag. judge of the ast. ct. of appeal, 1880-82; ag. atty.-gen. and ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ch. just. (on two occasions for special purposes) and Chancellor of the Diocese; sole comsrr. to report on a complaint of the Vestry of St. Michael (Barbados) against Bridgetown Water Works Co.; comsrr. to report on working of courts of inferior juris. (Barbados); comsrr. to investigate charges against rioters (Barbados); Grenada, 1883; judge of ast. ct. of appeal, 1883; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, 1884-5; local comsrr. of incumbered estates etc. (St. Vincent), Oct., 1884; comsrr. to examine and report on the govt. schl., St. Vincent; comsrr. to perform special duty in Grenada at the request of S. of S.; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888.

REES, LEWIS CHAS. MORRIS PARKER.—B. 1876; ed. Merchant Venturers Tech. Coll., Bristol; art. pupil to Messrs. Foster and La Trobe, Bristol, 1892; astt. to borough engrn., Islington, 1897; ch. astt. borough engrn., Margate, 1900; prin. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1902; also see, squatters' bd., 1905.

REEVE, HENRY, C.M.G. (1900), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; passed C.S. exam., Victoria, 1888; instd., Melbourne Univ., 1870; cadet, Victorian survey dept., 1873; cadet, N.S.W. survey dept., 1874; survr., 1878; survr., Fiji survey dept., May, 1880; staff survr., Jan., 1881; ag. comsrr. for wks., July, 1884, to Feb., 1885; col. engrn., St. Lucia, 1887; dir. of survey, Windward Is., 1891; mem. legis. coun., St. Lucia, 1891; special mem. exec. coun., St. Lucia and St. Vincent, 1891; col. engrn., Gambia, 1894; mem. exec. coun., 1895; ch. English comsrr., Anglo-French bindry. comsrr., 1895-6, 1898-9; director of pub. wks., Lagos, Aug., 1901; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May; ag. gov., May to July, 1902; ag. col. sec., 1902 and 1903; ag. gov., 1902; deputy gov., 1903; ret. 1st 4.

REEVES, HON. WILLIAM PEMBER.—Born Canterbury, N.Z., 1857; ed. in Colony; prov. scholarship holder, 1868, and 1872; N.Z. scholarships in classics and English, 1874; Somes Scholar, Christ's Coll., Canterbury; first elected mem. of H. of reprints, 1887; joined Ballance Min., Jan., 1891, holding the portfolios of educn., just. and labour; contd. in off. in Seddon Min. to Jan., 1896; author of following N.Z. Acts relating to labour:—Employers' Liability, Contractors and Workmen's Lien, Workmen's Wages, Conspiracy Law Amendment, Factories, Shipping and Seamen's Amendment, Shops and Shop Assistants Industrial Conciliation, Servants' Registry and Wages Attachment, the most important being Indust. Concil. Act, providing for compulsory arbitration in labour differences when negotiated between employers and employed fail: ass't for N.Z. in London, 1896; holds also the office of stock and loan agent for N.Z.; comsrr. of N.Z. Public Revenues Act; custodian of N.Z. Public Trust Fund Securities, &c., &c.; represented N.Z. at Internat. Commer. Congress, Philadelphia, 1899; mem. of Pacific Cable Co., 1899; high comsrr. for N.Z. in London, 1903; resigned, 1908, on apptmt. as mem. of London schl. of economies; mem. of London univ.; author of the "Long White Cloud," "State Experiments in Australia and New Zealand," and other publications relat. to N.Z.

REIBEY, THE HON. T.—Speaker of house assem., Tasmania, mem. for Westbury; mem. of the exec. coun., July, 1876; was leader of Opposition, Mar., 1875, to July, 1876; and in 1871, to Dec., 1878; premier and col. sec., July, 1876, to Aug., 1877; col. sec. in Mr. Crozier's ministry, Dec., 1878, to Oct., 1879; elected pres. July, 1887-1891; held off. without pay, 1894-99.
REID, ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (194).—Ed. gov't. ser., Tasmania, 1858; under treas. 1895.
REID, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—Ed. at Lees and Stafford; astt. store dept., Mersey, Liverpool, Jan., 1886; chief in 1889; astt. supt. (stores), Uganda, 1897; sen. astt. 29; chief storekeeper, 1903.

REID, THE RT. HON. G. H., P.C.—Min. of instrtn., N.S.W., Jan., 1883, to Mar., 1884; M.L.A. for E. Sydney; leader of Opposition, 1889-94; leader of Opposition in Commonwealth Parlmt. from the inauguration of the Commonwealth, 1901-4; premier and min. for external affairs, Aug., 1904, to Jan., 1915; represented N.S.W. in London at H.K. Diamond Jubilee.

REID, IRVINE KEMP.—M.B.C.M. (1884, M.I.H. (1887), D.P.H. (1st class honours in pract. hygiene) (1891), Abdn. Univ.; J.P. (194); mem. of Roy. Med. Soc., Edin.; mem. of Soc. of Offrs. of Health; house surg. and sec. to Norfolk and Lynn Hosp., King's Lynn; surg., res. med. staff, Col. Hospital, Freetown, Br. Guiana, 1887-89; govt. med. off. Is. of Wakenaam, Rio Essequibo, 1889; med. offr., penal sttlmt., Massaruni; and res. med. pub. hosp., Bartica, 1892; med. offr., Maracaibo, 1893; ditto, Peter's Hall dist., and mem. med. & 1894; med. offr., Cotton Tree dist., 1895.
REILLY, ROBERT O'MALLEY.—Sub-int. Bechuana land Prot. police, 1904.

RENDLE, ANSTRUTHER CARDEW.—Ed. Marlborough Coll., Christ's Coll., and St. Mary's Hosp.; B.A. (Camb.) 1888; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.); M.B., B.C., M.D., (Camb.), D.P.H.; joint bd., 1895; med. offr., E.A.F. and Uganda Prots., 12th Jan., 1906.

RENNER, WILLIAM, M.D. (Brux.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.K. and Q.C.P., L.M. (Ireland).—At the Liverpool Coll., and Univ. Coll., Lond., and Liverpool, Rotunda, Dublin, and St. Luke's Hosp., Brussels; mem. of the Pathologists Soc. of London; late ophthalmic asst. to Prof. Streatham, Univ. Coll. Hosp., Lond.; ag. sur. Leone, 1882-83; astt. col. surg., Freetown, in charge of lun. asyl., and incurable and smallpox hosp., Kissy; J. P., 1891; deputy coroner for dist. of Freetown, 1891; surg.-capt., late S. Le... vol. corps, and recd. W. African medal and clasp, 1898-99; has acted on several occasions as col. surg., pres. of quar. bd., inspr. of health, dep. harb.-mast., inspr. of health and shipping, and prin med. offr.

RENNIE, ALFRED HERBERT.—Ed. Hamilton Gram. Schl., and Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; confid. clk. to Hon. John Norquay, premier and prov. treas., Manitoba, 1885 to 1889; deleg. to England and New York to negotiate a loan for Manitoba, 1887; correspdee. clk., pub. wks. dept., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. ast. harbourm. and ag. supt., water pol., 1892; ag. sanitary supt., and sec., sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1893.

RENTON, ALEXANDER WOOD, M.A., LL.B. (honours), Edin.—Forensic prizeman; Lord Rector's essay prizeman; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1882; b. 1861; called to the bar, 1885; puisne judge, Mauritius, Aug., 1901; chmn., local comtce. of soc. of comp. legis., 1902; chmn. of mountain reserves comsn., 1934; ag. procureur and advocate-gen., Aug., 1904; puisne judge, Ceylon, Aug., 1905; author of "Wood Renton on Lunacy"; joint author of first edition of "Edmunds on Patents"; editor of Law Journal; editor of "Encyclopedia of the Laws of England"; editor of "The English Reports"; joint editor of 2nd edition of "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Laws."

RENWICK, SIR ARTHUR KT. BACH (1894), B.A. (Sydney), M.D. (Edin.), F.R.C.S.E.—B. 1837; min. of pub. instrtn., N.S.W., Feb., 1886; sec. for mines, Oct., 1881, to Jan., 1883; exec. comsnr. for N.S.W. at Melbourne Internat. Exhib., 1880-81; vice-pres., Amsterdam Exhibn., 1883; mem. of med. bd. of N.S.W., 1873; pres., state children's relief dept., 1881; fellow of the senate, Sydney Univ., 1872; pres., Sydney hosp. benevolent society of N.S.W., and of deaf, dumb, and blind asylums; comsnr. for N.S.W. at Chicago exhibition, 1893.
REYNOLDS, A. E.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900.

REYNOLDS, GEORGE LEWIS.—Ed. St. Matthew's higher grade schl., Manchester; served in 23rd Co. I.Y. (Duke of Lancaster's Own), 25th Jan., 1900, to 15th Nov., 1900; clk., dir. of rlyws. labour depot, Bloemfontein, O.R.C., 16th Nov., 1900, to 31st July, 1901; native refugee dept., 1st Aug., 1901, to 12th Mar., 1902; army pay corps, Bloem., 12th Aug., 1902, to 31st Oct., 1902; clk., C.S.A.R., Bloemfontein, Mar. to May, 1903; ch. clk. to controller, stat. dept., O.R.C., 9th May, 1903.
RHOADES, EDMUND LUSHINGTON.—Ed. Rugby Schl.; lieut. and comdr., naval dept., B. Cen. Africa Prot., 1894; 1st offr., marine transport dept., 1904.

RHODES, FRANK.—B. 1866; joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Sept., 1888; postmr. and telegraphist, Boshof, Sept., 1888; ditto, Bethlehem, Apr., 1893; ditto, Jagersfontein, Mar., 1895; 2nd supervisor, G.P.O., Bloem., Dec., 1895; inspr. of telegraphs, Jan., 1898; sec., posts and telegraphs, O.R.C., Mar., 1900; ag. P.M.G. and gen. man. of telegraphs, O.R.C., Feb. to July, 1901, and Mar. to July, 1906.

RICE, CECIL N.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; supernum. clk., admstr.'s office, St. Vincent, 1890; ag. 2nd clk., Apr. to July, 1891; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; 2nd clk., June, 1893; is also sec. to gen. bd. of health; ag. ch. clk., 1895; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. on several occasions, 1895-1901; assisted in relief work after hurricane, Sept.-Dec., 1898; port offr. and cust. offr., 1898; ag. sec., Hurricane Loan bd., 1899, 1900 and 1901; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is. (Grenada), Aug., 1901; ch. clk., Nov., 1901; relieving offr. and sec. to Capt. Young on special mission to St. Vincent after volcanic eruption, 1902.

RICHARDS, T. H. HATTON.—Associate to ch. justice, W. Australia, 1881; clk. to ch. judicial comsnr., Western Pacific, 1883; assoc. to ch. jus. and clk. sup. ct., Fiji, Nov., 1883; also marshal, vice-admiralty ct., 1884; ag. priv. sec. to Sir W. C. F. Robinson, govnr. of S. Australia, and clk. to exec. coun., July, 1889; A.D.C., May, 1886, and also in Melbourne, Mar., 1889; priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, admstr. of Br. N. Guinea, and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Aug., 1889; also a res. mag.; treas. and collr. of customs and a mem. of the exec. and leg. couns., 1890, and mem. native regulation bd. as 1st regsr.-gen., 1891, initiated a system of land registn. in the island and generally organised the dept.; agt. govt. sec., Feb. to Sept., 1892; asst. col. sec., Gold Coast, May, 1893; ag. ch. ast., col. sec., Mar. to July, 1894, and clk. to exec. and leg. couns. again from Sept., 1895, to Feb., 1896; clk. of couns., Oct., 1896, to Mar., 1897; invalided, 1897; asst. sec. to gov't. Cyprus, 1900; ag. com-nr., Larn ca dist., Aug. to Oct., 1904, and May to Oct., 1905.

RICHARDSON, EDMUND L.—Jun. clk., col. sec.'s office, Tobago, May, 1874; ch. clk., Oct., 1879; ag. regsr. of deeds, sup. ct., July, 1880, to June, 1881; ch. clk., audit office, July to Dec., 1881; 2nd rev. offr. and landing waiter, St. Lucia, Dec., 1881.

RICHARDSON, THE HON. EDWARD, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1831; entered the N. Zealand parlmt. in 1871 as representative for the city of Christchurch; min. for pub. wks., 1872; held that office through various changes of ministries till Jan., 1877, when he was compelled to retire on account of ill-health; again took office as min. of pub. wks., Sept., 1884, to Oct., 1887.
RICHARDSON, H. G.—B. 1884; entered C.O., Dec., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to bd. of educn.; transferred to C.O., Oct., 1902.
RICHARDSON, LT.-COL. HUGH.—Stip. mag. of N.W. Territories, Canada, 1876; presided at trial of Louis Riel, 1885; sen. puisne judge, sup. ct., N.W.T., 1887.
RICHARDSON, JAMES A.—Copyist in the regsr.'s office of Br. Guiana, Dec., 1878; 1st sworn clk., July, 1883.
RICHARDSON, JOSEPHUS HARGREAVES.—Entd. N. Zealand govt. insurance dept., 1874; comnr. of ditto since 1890.
RICHHEY, MATTHEW HENRY, K.C., D.C.L.—B. 1828; mayor of Halifax, N.S., 1864-7, and again 1875-8; sat in the commons for Halifax 1878-83; lieut.-gov. of N. S., 1883-8.
RICHMOND, JAMES, C.M.G. (1906), M.I.C.E.—Engnr. irrigation wks., Jamaica, 1874-90; dist. engnr., 1876-90; asst. dir. pub. wks., 1890-1900; director, Jamaica govt. rly., since 1900; is a J.P.

RICKETTS, OSWALD FREDERICK.—B. 1858; Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct., 1881; asst. res., 4th div., June, 1885; res., 2nd class, Jan., 1888; res. of Limbang, Mar., 1890; in charge of Lawas, Dec., 1905; and Truscan, May, 1906.

RIDDIKK, CHARLES.—Sub-inspr., Br. Guiana pol., 1894 to 1900; lieut., Sierra Leone batt. W.A.F.F., 1900 to 1904; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1904-1906; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 5th Jan., 1906; lieut., 4th batt. Essex regt., 1902; capt., 1906; instrs. (offrs.) certif., schl. of musk., Hythe, 1898; P.S. certif. (capt.), Chelsea, 1903.

RIDGEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sec., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; comnstr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comn. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal constitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comtee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908.

RIDLEY, A. A.—Clerical ast. in prisons dept., Br. Guiana, 10th Oct., 1878, to 31st Aug., 1887; 6th class offr. of customs, 1st Sept., 1887; 5th class, 1st Aug., 1888; 4th class, 12th Jan., 1896; 3rd class, 1st Aug., 1898.

RIDLEY, HENRY NICHOLAS.—M.A. Oxon.; ast. in botanic dept., Br. Museum, 1880 to 1888; dir. of gardens and forests, S. Stltmts., 25th Sept., 1888; sent on visit of inspection to Cocos-Keeling and Christmas Is., 1890.

RIES, BERNARD SAMUEL.—Asst. clk., immigr. dept., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1877; on spec. duty at off. of med. offr. to immigr. dept., Feb. to Mar., and Apr. to May, 1881; 5th cls. clk., Apr., 1891; 4th cls. clk., Mar., 1893; 3rd cls. clk., June, 1907.

RIGBY, WALTER.—Served with B.S.A. Pol., Matabeleland, 1886; Cape med. staff, Bechuana-land, 1897; 1 clasp; Impl. Light Horse and Kitchener's Fighting Scouts, Boer war; Queen's medal, 4 bars; King's, 2 bars, 1899-1902; inspr. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 16th May, 1904; asst. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 15th Oct., 1904.

RIND, WALTER LOCKHART.—B. 1876; ed. Rossall schl. and Univ. Coll., Oxon.; B.A. (hons.), 1899; priv. sec. and clk. to administrator, and clk. of councils, Seychelles, June, 1900; priv. sec. and clk. to gov., Nov., 1903; and supt. of printing, Jan., 1906; ag. aud., Oct., 1906; compiled Vol. III. of "Laws of Seychelles Revised, 1899-1907."

RIPON, 1ST MARQUESS OF (created 1871), GEORGE FREDERICK SAMUEL ROBINSON, K.G. (1869), P.C. (1863), G.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Son of 1st Earl of Ripon; born 1827; succeeded his father as Earl of Ripon and Viscount Goderich, 1859, and his uncle as Earl de Grey and Baron Grantham the same year; created a marquess in 1871, in recognition of his services as chmn. of the Joint High Comm., which arranged the Treaty of Washington in 1871; is lord-lieut. and C.A., N. Riding of York; dep. lord-lieut. of Yorkshire and Lincolnshire; under-S. of S. for War, June, 1859; removed to India Board, Feb., 1861; returned to W.O., July, 1861; and was S. of S. for War, Apr., 1863, to Feb., 1864, when he resumed his position at the India Board; finally resig., June, 1866; lord pres. of the comm., Dec., 1868, to Aug., 1873; gov.-gen. of India, 1880-4; first lord of the admiralty, Feb. to Aug., 1886; S. of S. for the cols., Aug., 1892, to June, 1895; lord privy seal, Dec., 1903; was M.P. for Hull, 1852-3; for Huddersfield, 1853-7; and for the W. Riding of York from 1857-9; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1870.

RISLEY, JOHN SHUCKBURGH.—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Lins of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L. 1894; author of "The Law of War," 1897; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th, 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1897.
RITTER, GUSTAVE A., C.M.G. (1902).—Appointed to the judge of the sup. ct. of Mauritius, regis. V.-A. ct. 1873; marshal of V.-A. ct., in unoffl. nominated mem. of legis. coun., May, 1894.
RIVERS, GEORGE F.—B. 1884; appntd. to compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the ser., and assigned to C.O., 16th July, 1902.

ROBB, DAVID.—B. 1860; 2nd cls. ast. shorthand writer, legis. assem., Natal, Feb., 1894; clk. ast. and Hansard reporter, legis. com., N. 1894; has acted as clk., legis. coun., and as ass't to govt. coms.

ROBERT, FREDERIC.—Admitted a solicitor, sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1885; ag. mag., Reing., from Nov., 1895, to Dec., 1896, June, 1897, Sept., 1898, and Apr., 1900, to June, 1901; dist. clk. and sen. civil status offr., P. Wilhelms, Curepipe division, 15th July, 1901; regis.-gen., May to Oct., 1903; ag. mag., rigues, 1st Apr., 1904; ag. dist. and sup. nat., Mauritius, 20th July, 1904; ag. mag., Dependencies, 19th Sept., 1904, confirmed dist. and sup. Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1907.

ROBERTS, ALFRED TEMPLE, M.A.—Ed. Charterhouse and Magd. Coll., Oxon.; 1st class in classical mods., and a 2nd class in classics finals; was "proxime accessit" for the first scholarship, and was awarded the "Greece" Greek prize in 1879; asst. master in Wyclif Coll., 1883; 1st class prof., Roy. Coll., Nat., 1886; sen. prof., 1901. ag. rector, Roy. Coll., 1903-04.

ROBERTS, CHARLES JAMES, C.M.G. (1871).—B. 1846; ed. at Sydney Gram. Schl.; adms. Sydney, 1877; J.P., 1878; mayor and cl. 1879; comnstr. at the Sydney Internat. Exhib., 1879-80; comnstr. for N.S. Wales at Melb. Exhib., 1880-81; at Amsterdam Exhib., 1881; at Calcutta Exhib., 1883-84; and at Col. Exhib., 1886-7; M.L.A., 1885 and 1889; gen., 1887; in the Parkes ministry; Centennial Celebration Comsn., N.S.W., 1888.
ROBERTS, J. C., F.I.P.S.—Offic. scr. writ., Barbados, June, 1895.

ROBERTS, JACOB THOMAS.—B. 1874; ed. C.M.S. Collegiate Schl., Lagos; joined civ. serv., apptd. 6th cls. clk., qtrmr's off., Haus., 3rd Aug., 1899; asst. storekeeper, med. dept., Jan., 1900; ag. med. storekeeper and warder, Leopold Hosp., 4th Nov., 1903 to 3rd Jan., 1904; clk., med. dept., 1st Apr., 1904; 3rd cls. audit off., 1st Feb., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, Jan., 1907; 1st cls. clk., ditto, 1st Jan., 1908; in charge of audit off., Ebute Metta, 21st to 26th Feb., 1908.
ROBERTS, ROBERT.—B. 1872; apptd. to serv., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1904.
ROBERTSON, DONALD.—B. 1860; clk., post office, N. Zealand, 1873, and filled various offices until his appt. as sec. of the post telegraph dept. in 1907.

ROBERTSON, W. C. F.—Ed. Trim. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1886 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1899; mem. of schls., G. Coast, 1898; director of educ., ag. comptroller of cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901.

ROBINSON, FRANKLIN.—B. 1878; M.A. Durham Univ., cadet, S. Stltmts., Oct., 1902; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, Apr., 1903; dist. education offr., Malacca, 4th Dec., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1904; mag., 1904; passed cadet, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Dec., 1905; ag. dep. regis. of sup. ct., sheriff, mag., comsrr. of ct. of requests, regis. of marriages, and chmn. of bd. of licensing justices, Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1908.

ROBINSON, J. Alex.—J.P.; and notary pub. for the col. (Newfield); ed. Vict. Coll., Jersey, and New Kingswood, Bath; prin. of Carbonbear gram. schl., 1883-92; elected for Bonavista, 1897; col. sec. of Newfield and mem. of exec. coun., 1897; accepted leadership of legis. coun., Dec., 1897; May, 1898, returned to H.A. for dist. of Trinity; mem. treasy, bd., 1898.

ROBINSON, James Reginald Wyndham.—B. 1880; ed. at Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods., 1901; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1903; apptd., after open exam., elk. in higher div., chief sec.'s office, Dublin, 18th Oct., 1904; 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 14th Aug., 1905; sec. to veterinary comttee, 1907.
ROBINSON, T. M.—Postmr., telegraphist, gaoler, and offr. for the sale of ammunition, Ixopo, Natal, Nov., 1883; sub-distributor of stamps, Nov., 1883; J.P. for Ixopo div., 1902.

ROBINSON, Victor George Mellor, J.P. (Natal).—Asst. teacher, ed. dep., Natal, 1st Feb., 1890; elk., Eshowe magistracy, 22nd Aug., 1892; gov'r's off., 1st Nov., 1892; treasy., 27th Oct., 1893; res. comsrr.'s off., 1st Oct., 1895; 1st elk., 27th Nov., 1897; 2nd cls. elk., chief mag. and civ. comsrr.'s off., prov. of Zululand, master, chief mag.'s ct., and regis., chief mag.'s and high cls., 30th Dec., 1897; sec. to chief mag. and civ. comsrr., 1st Oct., 1900; 1st cls. elk., 1st Nov., 1900; junior mag., 1st Jan., 1904; acted as mag., Eshowe; transf'd to treasy., 9th Jan., 1905; under-treas., 1st May, 1906; mem. civ. ser. bd.; sec. pub. debt comsrr.

ROBINSON, W. A.—B. 1874; ed. Appleby Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; 1st classical honours, 1893; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1895; B.A. 1895; 2nd cls. mod. hist., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1897; priv. sec. to Sir M. Ommanney, 1st Apr., 1905; 1st cls. elk., 10th Jan., 1907; asst. sec. to Impl. confee., 1907.
ROBINSON, Major W. Arthur.—Rov. Art., lieut., 1884; capt., 1893; temp. major, 1897; W. African Frontier Force, Dec., 1897.

ROBINSON, Sir William, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1877), F.R.G.S. —B. 1836; elk. in the C.O., Jan., 1854; asst. junior, Nov. 1854; 3rd class, June, 1858; was priv. sec. to Mr. Herman Merivale from June, 1858, to May, 1860; to Lord Blachford from May, 1860, to Mar., 1862, when he was promoted to be an asst. senior; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. Edward Cardwell from Apr. to June, 1864; apptd., Nov. 1869, to represent the C.O. on the E. African Slave Trade Comsrr.; sen. elk., July, 1870; prin. elk., Sept., 1872; one of the col. comttee for the Vienna Universal exhibn. of 1873, and special comsrr. for the crown cols., and supt. of the col. ct. at that exhibn.; edited the report upon the Br. cols. represented there; was one of the comttee for securing a permanent col. museum in London; lieut. gov. of the Bahama Is., Oct., 1874, and gov., 1875; lieut.-gov., Windward Is., 1880; gov., 1881; gov. of Trinidad, 1885; gov. of Hong Kong, 1891; received thanks of bd. of trade, 1877, and of H.M.'s gov't., Nov., 1878, in reference to administration of Bahamas; received also thanks of H.M.'s gov't., Dec., 1887, for satisfactory settlement of Venezuelan difficulty arising out of the "Henrietta" and "Josephina" cases; ret. 1898.

ROBISON, Chas. Carr.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and St. Paul's, Stony Stratford; served with several banking companies, etc.; attached to treasury, Sarawak, 1901; supt. of cust., 1902; postmr-gen., shipping-mast. and supt. of cust., 1904.
ROBSON, Wm. John.—Asst. aud.-gen., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; aud.-gen., 1st Sept., 1902.
ROCHA, J. G. da.—Entered the P.O., Hong Kong, Feb., 1865; acctnt., July, 1872; ag. asst. postmr., Sept., 1881, to May, 1883.
ROCKETT, E. D.—B. 1863; apptd., after a compet. exam., elk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1880; supt. of printing dept., 1894.
ROCKS, M. J. W.—Formerly elk. to various distr. comsrrs., S. Leone; afterwards gaoler, Gambia, 1891-8; 1st elk., traff. branch, S. Leone govt. rly., 1901.
RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1905.

RODEN, Robert Blair.—B. 1860; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; elk., public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd elk. to the gov., Leeward Is., 1881; acted on several occasions as ch. elk., and as elk. to the legis. and exec. coun., and as a dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., in 1883, 1890, 1895 and 1896; mag., coroner and J.P., Nevis, 1890; mem. St. Kitts and Nevis legis. couns., 1891; mem., fed. leg. coun., Leeward Is., 1898-9; mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1903; ag. judge, petty debt ct., Bridgetown, and judge asst., ct. of appeal, 1903-4; visiting justice, Glendairy pria., 1904.

RODESSE, Maurice.—B. 1871; Priv. sec. to several puisne judges and ch. justices, S. Stltlms., from 20th May, 1889; acted as dep. registr. or as sheriff and dep. registr., Singapore, on several occasions since 1893; confirmed as sheriff and dep. registr., 1st Jan., 1902; ag. offl. assignee in addition to own duties, Sept. to Oct., 1907.

RODGER, Sir John Pickersgill, K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1851; ed. at Eton and Ch. Ch., Oxon; called to the bar, Inner Temp. (certif. of honour), Jan., 1877; ch. mag. and comsrr. of lands, Selangor, Sept., 1882; ag. Br. res., Mar., 1884; Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1888; ag. res., Selangor, Apr. to Dec., 1891; res., Selangor, July, 1896; Brit. res., Perak, Sept., 1901; gov. Gold Coast Colony, 21st Sept., 1903.
RODWELL, Cecil Hunter.—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; served on staff of high comsrr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904.
ROE, Augustus S.—Called to the bar, Perth, W. Australia, 1872; special comsrr., sup. ct., 1872; pol. mag., Perth, Oct., 1897.

ROGER, Capt. Archibald.—Ensign 2nd roy. Lanark militia, Aug., 1899; gazetted to the 2nd W. I. regt., Nov., 1860; lieut., Aug., 1861; capt., Oct., 1866; exchanged to the 1st batt., 17th regt., Feb., 1867; sold out of the service, Jan., 1870; was priv. sec. to Mr. Bayley, gov. of the Bahamas, Dec., 1860, to July, 1863, and fort adjt., Jamaica, Dec., 1863, to Sept., 1864; pol. mag. of the rural dist., St. Christopher, June, 1873; dist. mag., May, 1874.

ROGERS, Claude Somerset.—B. 1867; obtained higher standard certif. at Imp. Forest Schl., Delhi Dun, India, with medals for forestry and forest engnrng., 1893; apptd. to Indian forest dept. as forest ranger, 26th May, 1893; promoted to extra asst. conserv. of forests, 4th grade, 26th Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Burmese language. higher standard, 1897; promoted to 3rd grade, 3rd Sept., 1897; 2nd grade, 27th Nov., 1899; forest officer, Trinidad, 24th Mar., 1901.

ROGERS, JAMES LUKE.—Joined the Leeward Is. pol., Nov., 1877; served in Antigua, Dominica, and Montserrat; corporal, Jan., 1888; ret., Nov., 1889; 3rd cl. warder, Montserrat gaol, Dec., 1889; ag. gaoler, June, 1898.

ROGERS, JOHN ARTHUR.—B. 1865; entd. P.W.D., Gold Coast, 4th Nov., 1893; offl. mem., Accra town coun., 12th Feb., 1901; appeal appraiser for town coun., Cape Coast, July, 1905; appeal appraiser for Sekondi and Accra town couns., Nov. and Dec., 19-6; served in Ashanti expedit., 1895-96; recd. thanks of headqtrs. staff; 2nd lieut., G. Coast vola., 28th July, 1899; lieut., 17th Aug., 1900; capt., 30th Oct., 1901; certif., army form E. 511, Chelsea barracks, Mar., 1900; inspr. of wka., 1st Jan., 1908.

ROGERS, THOMAS.—Employed by Cape govt., 1874 to 1889; by Main Reef G.M.C., Transvaal, 1889-90; in naval dockyard, Simon's Town, 1891-91; re-apptd. by Cape govt., Feb., 1891, to Mar., 1897; inspr. of drills and diamond setter, late O.F.S., Mar., 1897, to Oct., 1899; ditto, Imperial military govt., 1st Oct., 1900; ditto, civil govt., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902.

ROHRWEGE, FRANK, C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1859; ed. at Radley and Brasenose; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., hilary, 1885; joint author of "Parochial Assessment Rules"; a dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1894; ag. Queen's advoc., 1894; D.C., E. dist., 1895; pol. mag., 1886; resigned.

ROLANDO, FRANCOIS ADOLPHE.—B. 1845; regisr. and offr. of civil status, Seychelles, 6th Jan., 1890; ag. judge and stip. mag., 17th July, 1891; ag. stip. mag., 17th Nov., 1891; crown atty., Mauritius, 10th Sept., 1897; ag. recvr. of registn. dues and conservator of mortgages, 1st Oct., 1905.

RONAYNE, THOMAS.—B. 1848; ed. at Bath and Wakefield; employed on rly. construction in England; went to New Zealand in 1875, and apptd. man. of Kaipara rly.; transf. to Greymouth in similar capacity, 1876; res. and loco. engrnr. of Wellington Ma-terton rly., 1886; loco. engrnr., Hurunui-Bluff rly., 1888; transf. to Greymouth to re-organise Grey-Brunner rly., 1890; apptd. a rly. comsnr., 1894; gen. man. of govt. rlys., N. Zealand, 1895.

ROSBOROUGH, J.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A., Univ. of Dublin, 1893; capt. 6th R.I. Rifles, 1896; served with Gambia expedit., 1901 (medal with clasps); Sonalland, 19-2-1904 (clasps); Nandi expedit., B. E. Africa, 1905-1906 (ment. in desps., clasps); capt., K.A.R., Nyasaland Prof.

ROSE, JAMES FREDERIC.—B.1853; 1st cls., certf. schl. mast., B. Guiana; asst. mast., normal schl. for 2 years; elk. to inspr. of schl., July, 1873, to May, 1877; jun. educntl. dist. offr., May, 1877, to 1882; sen. educntl. offr., 1882 to 1891; 2nd asst. inspr. of schl., 1891 to 1903; acted as 1st asst. ditto on various occasions, 1892-19-3; 1st asst. ditto, July, 1903; ag. chf. inspr., 11th Mar. to 13th Sept., 1905; 2nd to 25th Jan., 1907; and from 16th Nov., 1907, to 22nd July, 1908.

ROSS, ALFRED ERNEST CLARENCE.—B. 1856; 2nd clk., surg.-gen.'s offr., Trinidad, 1875; clk. to dir. pub. wks., Feb., 1876; savings bk. clk., recvr.-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1880; acctnt., savings bk., Oct., 1882; sec. to trade and taxes comsn., 1886; acctnt., recvr.-gen.'s dept., May, 1889; ch. mag., savings bk., Jan., 1901; P.M.G., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903; ag. collr. of cust., Mar. to Dec., 1908.
ROSS, DAVID ALEXANDER.—Admitted to the bar, Quebec, 1884; Q.C., 1873; in 1878-9 mem. legis. assem. for co. of Quebec; mem. of exec. coun., atty.-gen., and in 1878-9, mem. legis. coun.

ROSS, GEORGE WM., LL.D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1841; held seat in house of commons, 1872 to 1883; Ontario legislature, 1883 to 1905; apptd. to Senate of Canada, 1905; min. of educ. 1883; was inspr. of pub. schls. from 1871 to 1883; comsnr. for Ontario and also for the Dominion to the Ind. and Col. exhib., 1886.

ROSS, JOHN KENNETH MURRAY.—Clerk asst. nativ. dept., Fiji, Sept., 1879; stip. mag., Aug., 1880; ag. res. comsnr., Colo West, Oct., 1882; mag., Tailevu and Ra, Apr., 1887; called to the bar, Fiji, Mar., 1888; ag. res. cons., Lomaiviti, Aug., 1888; proceeded in July, 1889, to Rarotonga and Penrhyn Is. as special judge, comsnr., under the W.P. high comsnr.; com. & customs, ch. inspr. of distilleries, etc., Dec., 1889; ag. atty.-gen., May to Sept., 1897; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1900; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1901, to Jan., 1902, and from May to Nov., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1903; mem. coun., Aug., 1903; M.L.C., 1905.

ROSS, PHILIP HEDGELAND.—Ed. at Otago Coll., New Zealand, Otago Univ., and St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P., Lond., 1900; D.P.H., Camb., 1902; bact.-ologist, E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Aug., 1903.

ROSS, REGINALD JAMES BLAIR, B.A.—R.1871; ed. at Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., Apr., 1894; dist. comsnr., Lagos, Mar., 1898; ag. pol. mag. on various occasions in 1898, 1899 and 1900; ag. asst. pol. mag., 1900; ag. res., Ibadan, Oct., 1901, to Sept., 1902; pol. mag., Jan., 1903; ag. atty.-gen., 1903, and 1905; ag. judge of sup. ct., Feb. to Mar., 1905; ag. atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, June to Oct., 1906; puisne judge, Cent. Prov., Oct. to Dec., 1906; again in 1907; comsnr. of Niger lands (Cent. Prov.), Apr., 1907.

ROSS, S. B. C.—Ed. Uppingham Sch. (1889-93); Owens Coll., 1893-96; B.A., 1896 (Eng. lang., lit. honours); univ. schlr., 1896; prize for Roman Law, Constitutl. Law, 1896; cadet, F.M.S., 1899; sent to Canton to study Chinese, 1900; transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; ag. regisr., land ct., 1902 and 1903; ag. asst. pol. gen., 1903; sec. to comsnr. of inquiry into est. regisr., 1903; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1903; P.M.G., 1903-4; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. to comsnr., 1904; ag. pol. mag., New Territories, 1905; ag. P.M.G., Apr., 1907; ag. asst. land offr., New Territories, Tai Po, Aug., 1907; ag. land offr., N.T., 2nd July, 1908.

ROSS, WILLIAM McCREGOR.—Ed. at Sctt. Port Gram. schl. and Liverpool univ.; M.S. (Hons. in engineering, Vict.), B.E., (Hons., B.R.U.I.), Assoc., M.Inst.C.E.; asst. engnr., Uganda rlyw., 18th Apr., 1900; D.P.W., E. Africa Prof., 1st Apr., 1905.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Dep't of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Lond.; hon. mem. anthrop. societies of Ber. and Florence, and of anthrp. inst., Lond.; chief protector of aborigines, Queensland; royal comsnr. to inquire into condition of nativ. of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of India and govt. med. offr., Pomeroon River, B. Guiana, 8th Dec., 1906.

ROTINI, J. F. AMOS.—Ed. Baptist elementary schl. and academy, Lagos; marine clk., Calabar, Oct., 1895; asst. regis. of ct., Calabar, June, 1896; dep. regis. of ct., Jan., 1899; trav. supervisor of native cts., S. Nigeria Prot., Sept., 1901, to Apr., 1907; ditto, E. Prov., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1907; has recd. thanks of govt. for services in connection with reorganization of native courts, S. Nigeria, 1902-3.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur., and King Henry VIII.'s Schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Term., 1891; licent. laws, Paris, 1891; ag. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Maur., Oct., 1899; ag. crown pros., Feb., 1900; crown pros., ag. advr. pol. mag., etc., Seychelles, 4th Sept., 1900; mag., Rodrigues, July, 1902.

ROUND, FRANCIS R., M.A., C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1845; ed. at Marl., and Balliol Coll., Oxford, where he obtained a double 2nd class in mods., and a 2nd in classics in final exam., 1868; apptd., after a compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for the Cols., Feb., 1869; 2nd class clk., Sept., 1872; accompanied Sir P. Julyan to Mauritius in 1873 as sec. on special mission of inquiry into the civil estabmt. and gen. expenditure of that col.; asst. priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon Sir Michael Hicks Beach, Mar., 1878; priv. sec., 1st Apr., to 27th Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., and financial clk., Dec., 1881; on special service to Br. Bechuanaaland and Mauritius, 1886; acted as col. sec., Mauritius, 1886-7; prim. clk., C.O., Mar., 1889; ret., 1905.

ROUPPEL, ERNEST P. S., D.S.O.—B. 1870; ed. at Marl. Coll.; 2nd lieut. R. Welsh Fus., 1888; transf. to Milford Haven div. R. Engnr. Militia, 1891; cons. agt., Oil Rivers Prot., 1893; transf. to Oil River Irreg., 1894; served in Benin R. expdt., 1894 (mentd. in desp.), and in minor operations; asst. comsnr., Anglo-German Bound. comsnr., 1896; political offr., 1897-8; divisional comsnr., Cross River div., So. Nig., 1898-1900; Ashanti expdn., 1900-1901; temporary 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 15th July, 1902; asst. sec. to govt. of Malta, 17th Apr., 1905.

ROUSSEAU, JAMES TOD, M.A., Edin.—B. 1860; ent. civ. ser., 1882; asst. sub-intendant of crown lands, Trinidad, 1897; magistrate and warden of Tobago, 1899; sub-intendant of crown lands, 1903; mag. and warden, Tobago, 1904.

ROUTH, REGINALD LAMY.—B. 1856; ed. St. Mary's Hall, Southsea, and Cadet School; frigate, H.M.S. "Conway"; extra master's certif., 1893; master of govt. vessels, Lagos, Apr., 1897; asst. marine supt., Lagos, Dec., 1902; ag. marine supt., Dec., 1903, to June, 1904; comdr., H.M.C.S.Y., "Ivy", So. Nig., June, 1906; sen. marine offr., E. Prov., S. Nig., May, 1907.

ROWDEN, ERNEST GEORGE.—B. 1874; special mast., roy coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1898; headmaster, Victoria schl., Seychelles, 27th Dec., 1901; inspr. of schl., Seychelles, 1st Mar., 1902; ditto, Gold Coast, 17th Dec., 1904; dir. of educn., 8th Feb., 1906.

ROWELL, THOMAS IRVINE, C.M.G. (1890), M.D., C.M., Aberdeen.—B. 1841; prin. civ. med. offr., S. Stlttns., July, 1877; and health offr., port of Singapore, J.P.; acted as col. surg. on several occasions from May, 1868, to Mar., 1876; accompanied Sir W. Robinson on mission to invest King of Siam with G.C.M.G., Nov., 1878; regis. of births and deaths, Dec., 1882, and of Mohammedan marriages, Dec., 1883; pres. fisheries inquiry comsn., 1887; pres. Singapore mun. coun., 1888-9; ret. 1890.
ROWLAND, J. W., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1852; asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., May, 1880; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1887; col. surg., 1887; retired, 1897.

ROWLEY, THOS. WM.—B. 1859; ed. Chelt. Coll.; 2nd lieut., 2nd L.I. Regt., Roy. Guernsey Militia; attached to 105th Regt., L.I., at Guernsey, for two trainings, and passed school; lieut., Mar., 1879; passed prelim. exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, Apr., 1880, and qualifying exam. for line, July, 1881; joined armed constabty., N.Z., 1885; transf. to mtd. pol., Jan., 1886; and to N.Z. Artill., June 1886; resig., Aug., 1888; inspr. of roads, Perak, Nov., 1889; asst. collr. land rev., mag. and J.P., Perak, Dec., 1890; ag. mag., Matang, June, 1892; asst. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Matang, Jan., 1894; ag. supt. of prisons, Apr., 1895; asst. mag., Tanjong Malini, 1898; asst. comsnr. of pol., Selangor, 1899; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1900; dist. treas. for different districts, Jan., 1903, to Feb., 1904; asst. dist. offr., Matang, Apr., 1905; mag., Taiping, Apr., 1906; dist. offr., coast, Negri Semilan, Jan., 1907.

ROXBURGH, T. LAURENCE.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; clk. petty sess., St. Eliz., Jamaica, 1st Jan., 1882; of cts., 1st Apr., 1888; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Jan., 1890; sen. clk., 1899; asst. col. sec., 2nd Jan., 1902; acted col. sec., Jamaica, 29th Mar. to 10th Apr., 1902; 1st July to 21st Nov., 1902; 2nd June to 10th Sept., 1903; Jan. to Mar., 1904; 25th May to 9th June, 1904; and 9th June to 10th Oct., 1906; was a major in Jamaica militia, and was Hon. A.D.C. to Gov. Sir A. Hemming, 1898 to 1904; also acted as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, Mar. to Aug., 1901; admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1906.
ROZAN, JULIEN.—Spcl. clk., audit. off., Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1870; ch. clk., ditto, 19th June, 1897.
RUSHMER, JAMES.—B. 1876; entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896.
RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY.—Ck., comsnr. of rlyws. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comsnr. of rlyws., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901.

RUSSELL, Lieut.-Gen. Sir BAKER CREED, G.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1880), 13th Hussars.—B.1837; was in command of the troops employed against Sikukuni in S. Africa; A.D.C. to Her Majesty Queen Victoria, 1880; asst. adjt.-gen. for cavalry in the expdn. to Egy., 1882, where he also commanded the 1st Cavalry brigade.

RUSSELL, JAMES GEORGE, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1848; master of the sup. ct., S. Australia, Dec., 1878; regis. of V.-A.ct. of companies, of trades unions, 1878; comsnr. of inland rev. and ag. regis. of probates, 1884; comsnr. of taxes, 1884; of stamps, 1886; comsnr. of insolvency, 1889; special mag. local ct., Adelaide, 1889; pres. of state bd. of conciliation, 1895; ag. judge of sup. ct., Mar. to Oct., 1897.
RUSSELL, JOHN WILLIAM PEMBERTON.—Asst. transport offr., Uganda Prot., 21st Aug., 1899; chief of customa, Apr., 1904; transport offr., 1905.

RUSSELL, ROBERT, JUN.—B. 1867; Natal exhbr. from Pietermaritzburg high schl. to Merton Coll., Oxford, 1885; B.A., M.A., and B.C.L., 1893; called to the bar by the Inn, Tem., 1893; acted as clk. in atty.-gen.'s office, Natal 1891; asst. impr. of schl., 1892; sec. to the agt.-gen. for Natal in London, Dec., 1893; acted as priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. H. Escombe, ex-premier of Natal, during his visit to England, 1897; rep. Natal, along with the agent-gen., on exec. comttee. of S. African products exhib., London, 1897.

RUSSELL, WESTERN FRANCIS, B.A.—Ed. at Cranbrook schl. and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd cls. math. mods.; 1st cls. math. finals, 1884; 1st cls. prof. (math.) Royal coll., Mauritius, Aug., 1886; ag. sen. prof., 12th Apr. to 28th Nov., 1894; and 1st Aug., 1901, to 17th Oct., 1902; ag. rector, 18th Oct. to 30th Nov., 1902; ag. sen. prof., 27th Mar., 1903, to 8th Feb., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec., 24th Feb., 1904.
RUTLEDGE, THE HON. SIR A., KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1843; barrister, Queensland, 1878; mem. of legis. assem. since 1878; atty.-gen. from 1883 to 1888; dist. ct. judge, Queensland, 1906.

RYAN, SIR CHARLES LISTER, K.C.B. (1887), C.B. (1881), J.P., Berks.—B. 1831; apptd. asst. examr., impl. audit office, Mar., 1851; jun. clk. in H.M.'s treasry., Whitehall, Feb., 1852; was priv. sec. to Mr. Disraeli, Sir Stafford Northcote, and Mr. Gladstone; apptd. to audit office as sec., May, 1865; asst. comptroller and auditor, Mar., 1873; comptroller and audr.-gen., June, 1888; was hon. auditor of certain crown cols., ag. on behalf of H.M.'s S. of S. for the cols.; ret. 1896.

SABBEN, FREDERICK.—B. 1854; ed. Royal Naval Schl.; govt. agt., Fiji, July, 1882; inspr. of imigts., Jan., 1883; boarding offr., Nov., 1883; bondkeeper, 2nd class clk., and magazine-keeper, Jan., 1884; inspr. of pol., and offr. in charge of gaol, May, 1886; gaoler, Aug., 1887; supt., prisons, Aug., 1903.
SACHESE, HON. ARTHUR OTTO, C.E.—M.L.C. for N.E. Prov., Victoria; min. of pub. instr. since Sept., 1903.

SADLER, SIR JAMES HAYES, K.C.M.G. (1907), C.B. (1902).—Lieut.-Col. Indian Staff Corps; late foreign dept., govt. of India; 1st comsln., 6th July, 1870; served with 61st Foot in Canada and Ireland, and 40th Foot and 33rd Bengal Native Infantry in India; apptd. to polit. dept. in India, July, 1877; asst. agt. to gov.-gen. at Baroda, May, 1881; asst. sec. legis. dept., July to Oct., 1886; Aug. to Oct., 1888, and Apr., 1889, to Dec., 1890; polit. agt. with the ex-Amir of Afghanistan, Apr., 1891; agt., Kotal and Jhalawar, July, 1891; on spec. duty with Prince Damong of Siam, Feb., 1892; polit. agt. and consul at Muscat, Nov., 1892; ag. res. and consul-gen., Persian Gulf, June, 1893; asst. sec., govt. of India. foreign dept., Apr., 1895; polit. agt. and consul, Somaliland Prot., Aug., 1898; ret. from the Indian serv., Mar., 1899; continued to be employed as consul-gen., Somaliland, till 1901; consnr., Uganda Prot., Dec., 1901; consnr. and comdr.-in-chief, E. Africa Prot., 11th Dec., 1905; govt. and comdr.-in-chief, E. Africa Prot., 9th Nov., 1906.

SAINTSBURY, JOHN SEYMOUR.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; 1st cls. in Evidence, Procedure and Criminal Law, bar exams., 1904; certif. of honour from council of legal educ. at Bar Final, 1905, and a prize of 50l., from the Inn; passed civ. ser. test exam., Barbados, 1882; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., 1883; in P.O., 1884; 7th clk., P.O., June, 1886; 4th clk., audit off., Sept., 1890; offr. of cust., Dec., 1890; inland rev. offr., Oct., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," July to Nov., 1906.
SALKELD, ROBERT EDWARD.—Late shire Light Infantry, comdy. comdr., 3rd div. collr., E. Africa Prot., 12th Jan., 1906.
SALOMONS, SIR JULIAN EMANUEL KT. BACH. (1891).—B. 1834; vice-pres. privy. coun., N.S. Wales, 1891; resig., 1893; mem. legis. coun.; London agent-gen., N.S.W., 1900.
SAMPSON, VICTOR, K.C.B., R.A., LL.D.—Obtained Cape Univ. Chanc. gold medal for on the native question, 1877; M.A., Colony, 1898-1908; atty.-gen., 1904-8.

SAMUEL, SILVERIO, I.—Ed. at St. Greg. Rom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; messr. and educ. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to inspr. of schools, 1899; sec. to comttee. for exhibit of wks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter, comsln., Ilesha interior depot, Feb., 1904; cls. clk., govr.'s off., Aug., 1904.

SAMUELSON, S. O.—Ag. regiar. natire ct., Natal, Feb., 1881, to Apr., 1882; intpr., sup. ct., May to June, 1882; clk. and interp. to R.M., Umlazi div., Oct., 1882; interp., sup. ct., Feb., 1888; under-secr. for affairs, 1893; mem. of civ. ser. bd.: 1894.
SANDERS, WM. RUTHERFORD BOGLE, Ceylon, 4th Oct., 1879; pol. mag., 1st Jan., 1880. list, judge, 1st May, 1896; ag. govt. agt., N. prov., 21st Apr., 1902; officer of cls. II., 18th July, 192.

SANDERSON, JAMES.—Ck. to ch. just., Barbados, 1867; clk. to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, Apr., 1871, also clk. to quarantine bd., in 1880; solr., atty., and proctor, Barbados, in 1873; was deleg. for Barbados to quarantine confce. at Demerara in 1888; govt. dir. on bd. of Barbados rly. co. in 1891; commr. of probates, 1904; J.P., 1904.
SANDERSON, STANLEY.—3rd treasy, asst., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1901; supt. of inland rev. and conservancy, 6th Feb., 1903.
SANDIFORD, CHARLES, C.B. (1903), M.L.A.E., M.I.M.E.—Loco. supt., N.W. rly., India; loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1899.

SANGSTER, GUY HENRY.—B. 1871; served in 2nd Dragoons and 91st Highlanders; asst. supt. pol., Gambia, 1897; ag. supt. of pol., inspr. of prisons, Gambia, 1897; asst. inspr. S. Leone frontier force, 1898; Mendy expdh., 1898, mentioned in despatches; ag. dist. comsrr., Karene, 1899; inspr., S. Leone F.F., June, 1899; W. Africa medal and clasp, S. Leone, 1898-9; Royal Humane Soc.'s bronze medal, 1898; asst. dist. comsrr., S. Leone, Dec., 1899-1900; travelling comsrr., Gambia, Nov., 1900; J.P. and comsrr. of ct. of requests of the col.; field treas., Gambia field force, 1901; special mention in despatches; W. African general serv. medal and clasp; priv. sec. to ag. govt. of Gambia; promoted 2nd cls. comsrr.

SANKEY, JOHN.—B. 1867; customs clk., England, Dec., 1886; excise offr., Jan., 1887; 2nd div. clk. in acctnt.-gen.'s dept., June, 1887; ag. asst. P.O. survr., 1897; acctnt., dept. of posts and telegraphs, O.R.C., Sept., 1901; ch. acctnt., ditto, July, 1903.

SARWAR, HAFIZ GHULAM.—Cadet, S. S., Sept., 187; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan. to June, 1890; and from Sept., 1900, to Nov., 1901; passed final exam., Chinese, July, 1900; 3rd mag., Sing., 29th Apr., 1902; ag. sheriff, deputy-registr., and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1905; asst. registr., sup. ct., Malacca, May, 1907; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1908.
SASSE, HAROLD ST. JOHN.—Ck. coll. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 1st Aug., 1902; clk., audit off., Hong Kong, 21st June, 1905.

SAUER, THE HON. W.—Sec. for native affairs in Sculeni ministry, Cape Col., May, 1881; ret. 1884; col. sec., July, 1890, to Jan., 1896; comsrr. of pub. wks., Oct., 1898, to 1900; mem. for George, 1901; min. of pub. wks., 1908.

SAUNDERS, CHARLES JAMES, B.A.—B. 1868; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., May, 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Mar., 1897; dist. offr., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., and supt. of Ind. immigr., Penang, and munici. comsrr. for Penang, 1898; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1899; also ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Sept., 1898; 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, Aug., 1898; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1900; ag. offr. assignee, S. S., Sept. to Dec., 1900, Jan., 1901, to Feb., 1905, and Feb., 1906; sheriff, dep. registr., supreme court, and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; munici. comsrr. for Sing., Oct., 1900, and Sept., 1903; also ag. prot. of Chin., Sing., Apr., 1904; also asst. supt. of Indian immigr.; asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, May, 1904; ag. sec. for Chin. affairs, S. S. and F. M. S., Apr., 1907; ag. comsrr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1908; ag. dist. judge, Apr., 1908.

SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906, C.M.G., 1895.—B. 1857; held various apptmts. in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (desps.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand, 1889 (desps.); variously employed under Zululand Govt., 1888-95 (desps.), when he was apptd. spec. comsrr. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. comsrr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. comsrr. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Coms., 1902, and comsrr. for native affairs, 1905.

SAUNDERS, SIR FREDERICK RICHARD, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1838; asst. survr., Ceylon, 1857; asst. agt., Trincomalee, July, 1862; asst. to govt. agt. at Kandy, Aug., 1865; dist. judge, etc., Ratnapura, Nov., 1865; asst. to the govt. agt., W. Prov., at Ratnapura, Oct., 1867; inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1874; agt., E. Prov., 1878; agt., W. Prov., 1879; M.L.C., 1879; ag. audr.-gen., 1890; treas. and comsrr. of stamps, Oct., 1890; ag. audr.-gen., 1896; ret., 1898.

SAUNDERS, JAMES BERNARD.—B. 1852; harbrmr., Trinidad, Sept., 1894; also J.P. and mag. under harbr. and drogher ordcs., regis. of births and deaths for the waters of the Col., coroner under harbrs. ordce., supt. of lighthouses, mem. of quar. authority, comsrr. of pilotage, and supt. of mercantile marine.

SAUNDERS, REGINALD G.—Ed. at Tonbridge and Cheltenham Coll.; ag. 3rd asst. P.M.G., Ceylon, 13th Jan., 1898; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and asst. collr. of cust., 1st June, 1901; pol. mag., Matara, 22nd Apr., 1903; extra asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1st Mar., 1904; ag. asst. govt. agt., Manaar, 2nd May, 1904; comsrr. of requests and pol. mag., Chilaw, 12th Feb., 1906; ag. asst. govt. agt. and dist. judge, 11th Apr., 1906; ag. asst. collr. of cust., Trincomalee, Sept., 1908.
SAUVAGE, DANIEL.—Govt. pilot and asst. port offr., Seychelles, Feb., 1894, and port offr., May, 1897.
SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—Pol. probationer, S. Stltlbs., Nov., 1904; asst. supt. of pol., Jan., 1908.

SAXTON, GEORGE SHADWELL.—B. 1858; ed. at Clifton Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., 1878; pol. mag. Haputale, 1887; asst. govt. agent, Mannar, 1887; Chilaw, 1888; dist. judge, Tanga-la, 1890; asst. govt. agent, Matale, 1890; Matara, 1899; Kalutara, 1902; govt. agent, Sabaragamawa, 1902; dist. judge, Matara, Nov., 1906; govt. agent, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1906; ditto, Kurunegala, Oct., 1907.

SAYER, W. F.—Ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., Lond.; solr., 1879; bar. and solr. in W. Australia, 1891; sec. law dept., Mar., 1897; ag. comsrr. of titles, Mar., 1899; atty.-gen., 1901; parly. draftman, 1902; crown solr., Dec., 1902; atty.-gen., 1904.

SCANLEN, THE HON. SIR THOMAS CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1834; mem. house of assmnt. Cape since 1869; dep. sheriff for Cradock, 1867-66; J.P. for Cradock, May, 1869; mem. of the divisional coun. of Cradock, 1867-77; comn. and chmn. of the municipality of Cradock, 1870-77; apptd. by sup. ct. examin. for admission of attorneys and notaries, May, 1887; mem. of the exec. coun., May, 1881; atty.-gen. and premier, May, 1881, to June, 1882; col. sec. and premier, July, 1882, to May, 1884; proceeded to England to negotiate loan for pub. wks., Oct., 1883; as a priv. mem., carried through the Executive and Trustees Accounts Act, 1873; Titles Registration and Derelict Lands Act, 1881; prepared and carried during term of office, General Loans Act, 1881; Postal Act, 1882; Lands Clauses Act, 1882; Municipal Act, 1882; Police Regulation Act, 1882; Aliens Naturalisation Act, 1883; Public Health Act, 1883; Liquor Licensing Act, 1883; legal adviser, Rhodesia, 1894; legal adviser, Rhodesia Administrative Staff, 1895.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILFRED JOCKLYN STUART.—Bechuanaland Border pol., Jan., 1891; line mag., Bechuanaland rly. construction, 1st Feb., 1896; rea. mag., Ngamiland, 22nd Jan., 1897; native comn., N.W. Transvaal, 22nd June, 1902; served in Matabele campaign, 1893; Matabele rebellion, 1896; S. Africa, siege of Mafeking, etc.; A.D.C. to Lieut.-Gen. Lord Methuen, etc.; comdt., North Prot., and lines of communication, 1st Mar., 1902; mentioned in despatches; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 23rd June, 1906.

SCHOLTZ, CLERKE BURTON.—Comn., Lichtenburg, Transvaal, 1877; lieut., Bechuanaland field force on Sir C. Warren's staff; mem. of comn. on Stellaland claims, 1885; ch. clk. and asst. mag., Vrijburg, 1885; R.M., Kuruman, 1889.

SCHOMBURGK, OTTO HEINRICH.—Clk., engr.-in-chief's office, S. Aust., Jan., 1874; ch. clk. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; clk., regis.-gen. of deeds office, July, 1880; clk., sheriff's office, Nov., 1882; dep. sheriff, 1891; also returning off. W. Adelaide, Jan., 1893, to June, 1902; sheriff, July, 1903; also Commonwealth electoral offr., for the state of S. Aust., Aug., 1903; major, comdg. field batt. of artillery (retired).

SCHOLES, SIR HENRY RAWLINS PIPON, KT., B.A. (1905).—B. 1850; ed. Marlborough; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Hilary, 1873; admitted to bar, Leeward Ia., May, 1873; mem. for Sandy Point legis. assem. of St. Kitts, Mar., 1874, to Apr., 1876; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Ia., Apr. to Sept., 1876; re-elected mem. for Sandy Point, Jan., 1877; mem. of new legis. coun. of St. Kitts, 1879, and elected by the legis. coun. as sen. mem. for St. Kitts of fed. coun. of the Leeward Is.; June, 1880, atty.-gen., Br. Honduras; June, 1881, apptd. provisionally as ch. just. of Br. Honduras; atty.-gen., Grenada, Apr., 1883; ditto, Jamaica, 1896; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1905.

SCHREIBER, COLLINGWOOD, C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1831; emigrated from England, 1852; entered service of Hamilton and Toronto Rly., and became asst. engr., 1852; supting. engr. of wks., N. Rly. of Canada, 1860-64; div. engr., Nova Scotia govt. service, on Truro and Picton Rly., 1864-5; survey engr., Dominion govt. service, on inter-col. rly., 1886; supting. engr., N. Brunswick E. Rly., 1869-71; ditto, inter-col. railway, 1871-3; ch. engr. of govt. railways in operation, 1873-8; also gen. man., 1878-80; also engr. in-chief, Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; nat. engr. in-chief, govt. rlys. and dep. min. of rlys. and canals.

SCHREINER, THE HON. W. P., C.M.G. (1890), K.C.—B. 1857; ed. S. A. Coll., Capetown, Camb. Univ., and London; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape, 1882; legal adviser to high comn., 1887-93; atty.-gen. in Mr. Rhodes's ministry, 1893; mem. of James Raid Comttee; premier and col. sec., 1888-1894; M.L.A., Queenstown, 1908.
SCOFFHAM, W. H.—B. 1883; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. sec. and assigned to census office, 20th Feb., 1901; transfd. to C.O., 17th Dec., 1902.

SCONCE, HERBERT W.—Ed. at Hurst Col., Sussex; Gram. Schi., Bristol; Trim. Coll., Camb. B.A. math. tripos, 1886; M.A. 1897; called to bar, Inner Temple, July, 1908; asst. master, Paversham Gram. Schi.; Godolphin Schi., Harmersmith, and Grocers' Co.'s Schi., Hackney, 1898 to 1893; asst. mr., Queen's Coll., Br. Gdns., Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. of schls., July, 1896; ag. inspr. May to Nov., 1898; June to Nov., 1900; July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; inspr. of schl. as off. visitor of Onderneeming Indust. Schi., Apr., 1903; mem. bd. of agric. for educat'n. matters examin. for prelim. exam. for govt. clerical serv.

SCOTT, J. H.—Mag., Tembuland, Aug., 1876; capt. in Tembuli levies, Sept., 1877; ag. ch. nat. Tembuland, Apr. to July, 1878; ch. clk., nat. affairs dept., Oct., 1878; sec. to atty. gen., special duty to N. Border during war there, Oct. to Apr., 1879; ag. special comn., N. Border, to July, 1879; special comn., and special of N. Border, July, 1879; res. comn., Port Elizabeth, July, 1888.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B. 1874; B.A. Lond. Univ. cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam., Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. duty, July, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1906; ag. comn., ct. of requests, Singapore, 1906 to Feb., 1907; dist. offr., Dindings, 1906; collr. of land rev., Penang, Feb., 1906; ag. comn., ct. of requests, Singapore, Dec., 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1908; ag. comn., ct. of requests, Malacca, Oct., 1908.

SCOTT, THE HON. RICHARD WILLIAM, KT.—Called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1884; called a Q.C., 1867; sat in the legis. assem. of Ont. from 1867 to 1873, and while there carried into effect the Separate School Act of Upper Canada; returned to the Ontario assem. at confedn., 1867; ret. his entrance into the Dominion Govt., Nov., 1874; called to the senate, Mar., 1874; was speaker of the Ontario assem. from 7th to 21st Dec., 1874; mem. of exec. coun. and comn. of crown land for same prov., from later date until Nov., 1878, when sworn of the Queen's privy coun.; S. of S. of Canada, Jan., 1874; ret. on resignation of Mackenzie govt., Oct., 1878; is now a senator; was the framer of the Canada Temperance Act known as the Scott Act; joined Sir W. Laurier's govt. as S. of S., June, 1896.
SCOTT, ROBT.—B. 1873; acctnt. to direct. army telegraphs, O.R.C., July, 1900; asst. acctnt. dept. of posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1902.
SCOTT, WALTER.—B. 1862; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd div.) of the civ. serv., and assigned to the C.O., 25th Jan., 1883; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., 3rd Nov., 1896.

SCOTT, WM. ALEXANDER.—B. 1871; clk. to atty.-gen., Fiji, 15th Oct., 1888; ag. stip. mag., Navua and Nadroga, tax inspr., Oct., 1897; stip. mag. and asst. comn., Colo West, 1888; clk., native dept., and stip. mag., Kadavu, 1897; prov. inspr., Kadavu and Lomaviti, 1901; stip. mag., Rewa, and comsnr., Naitasiri, 1902; ag. stip. mag., Navua, and comsnr., Namasi, 1903; asst. native comsnr., 1904; despatched to Christchurch exhibition in charge of body of Fijian natives, 1906-7; ag. chief pol. mag. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, 1907; dep. native comsnr., 1908.
SCROGGS, Lieut. Harold Chas., R.N.—Harbour mast. and supt. of merc. marine, Mauritius, 23rd June, 1903.

SCRUBY, Charles Burdett, B.A.—B. 1876; ed. priv. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb., and in France; asst. collr., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1889; ag. vice-consul, Fort Johnston, 1901; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1902; ag. collr. of cust., 1904; asst. to comsnr. for Egba hndry. delimitation, 1904-5; certif. from schl. of mil. engnr., Chatham, in mil. topography and astronomy, 1905; passed Yoruba exam., 1906.
SEALY, A.—Pol. mag. of dist. B, Barbados, Sept., 1873.
SEALY, Frank Dean.—Supernum. clk., immigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1884; 4th class clk., Apr., 1891; 3rd class, Jan., 1894; 2nd class, Apr., 1895; immigrn. agt., Oct., 1898.

SEALY, Gordon Courland Miller.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confdl. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg.-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treasy. dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, James Ernest Cameron.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to comsnrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasy., June, 1893; postmtr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; clk. to sub-recvr., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treasy., Tobago, Feb., 1904.

SEARCY, Arthur.—Boarding offr., cust., S Aust., 1873; clk. cust., 1874; clk., audit offr., 1875; clk. cust., 1876; tide inspr. and measurer of ships cust., 1889; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comsnr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; clk. asst. and sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., 1894; comsnr. of taxes and dep. comsnr. of taxes and stamps, Oct., 1897; also pres., marine bd., 1902; also inspr. of kerosene, 1902; comsnr. of trade marks and regsr. of copyrights, 1904-7; also controller, outer harbour, Port Adelaide, Nov., 1907.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SECCOMBE, George Samuel R.L.C.P.(Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1874—B. 1852; mem. Medico-Psychological Assoc.; sen. asst. med. offr. and dep. med. supt. of Caterham Asylum, England, 1874; med. supt. of lun. asyl., and med. offr., roy. gaol, Trinidad, Sept., 1882.
SEDGEWICK, Robert, K.C.—Dep. min. of just. and solr. of Indian affairs, Canada, 1888; now a puisne judge, sup. ct.

SEELY, Lieut.-Col. John Edward Bernard, D.S.O. (1906).—Ed. at Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (depa., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-1906; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, since 1906; recd. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life at sea, 1891; parly. under-secr. of state for the colonies, Apr., 1908; chmn. of comte. to inquire into organisation of crown agents' office, 1908.

SEIGNORET, Clarence Augustus.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yaws comsn., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., regist. a office and clk. pub. market, 1892; asst. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treasy., 1895; sec., bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harbmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmtr., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., regist.'s off., 1895; ag. postmtr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2nd Earl of (creat. 1882), Viscount Wolmer, Baron Selborne (U.K.), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), The Rt. Hon. Wm. Waldegrave Palmer.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cols., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comte., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comte., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high comsnr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 15th Mar., 1905; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of rep. govt., June, 1907.

SELBY-LOWNDES, R. C. W.—Ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Tamworth Coll.; served in B.N.A. pol. during Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-7; chartered company's civil serv., 1888-1900; served during S. Africa war, Rhodesia regt., Rhodesian field force, and French's scouts, 1900-1902 (medals, Mashonaland, 1897, Queen's medal, S. A. war, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps).

SELLS, H. C., B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S. Sttltns., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1:00; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. asst. postmtr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B, Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1903; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regist., Leeward Is., and regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., regist. of titles, regist. of friendly societies, and regist.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and regist. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905.
SENEVIRATNE, Alexander de Alwis.—Mem. of legis. coun., Ceylon; advoc. of the sup. ct.; mem. mun. coun., Colombo, and coun. of legal educn.; chmn. Ceylon national assoc.

SENIOR, Bernard, I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1865; clk. to the budry. comsnr. (Redistribution of Seats Act), 1884; attached to Scotch educn. dept., 1885, and again 1888; priv. sec. to Sir F. R. Sandford, under-sec. for Scotland, 1887; 1st class elk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1888; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ditto, G. Coast, Dec., 1889; ditto, Br. Bechuanaland, Feb., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1895; aud.-gen., O.R.C., Sept., 1902; is also chmn. of tender bd.; mem. of comsn. to inquire into law relating to mining for diamonds and precious stones in O.R.C., 1903; ag. col. treas., 12th Aug., 1903, to 15th Feb., 1904; M.L.C., May, 1904; deleg. for O.R.C. at S. African freights confce., 1904-5; mem. of O.R.C. civ. ser. comsn., 1905; chmn. of govt. tender bd.; dir. of National Bank of O.R.C.; col. auditor, Ceylon, 1st Mar., 1907; author of "A Guide to the examination of Government Accounts in Cyprus."
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief acct., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SETH, ARATHOON, I.S.O. (1905).—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1893; Hindustani interp., magistracy, Hong Kong, Feb., 1868, to Dec., 1869 (in the serv. of the P. & O. Co. in the interim); re-entered magistracy as 3rd elk., Sept., 1872; 2nd elk., Sept., 1873; 1st elk., Feb., 1875; elk. of couns. and ch. elk., C.S.O., Feb., 1881; valuer under the "Rating Ord.", 1875, 25th June, 1881, to 19th Mar., 1883; J.P., Jan., 1882; sup't. of the opium revenue, Aug., 1883, to Feb., 1885; ditto, imports and exports, June to Oct., 1887; sec. to bd. under "Taipingshan resumption ordce." 1894-1895 (reed. thanks of govt.), sec. to insanitary properties comsn., July, 1896; ag. deputy land offr., Nov., 1894, to Jan., 1895; ditto, Mar., 1895, to Nov., 1896; dep. registr. and appraiser, sup. ct., and comsnr. for oaths, Mar., 1895; comsnr. for taking acknowledgments of married women, Oct., 1896; sec. squatter's bd.; offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1895, to Nov., 1896; ag. ass't. registr.-gen., July to Sept., 1897; ag. registr., sup. ct.; ag. land offr.; ag. registr. of companies; offl. admstr., and offl. trustee, 15th Dec., 1898, to 26th Jan., 1900; mem. bd. examrs., 1902; ag. registr., sup. ct., offl. admstr., offl. trustee and registr. of companies, 1903-04; confirmed, 1904.

SETHI, G. G.—B.A., Calcutta; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1901; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1903; passed final exam in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906; asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907.

SEVERN, CLAUD.—B. 1869; B.A. Cantab.; temporary elk. in librarian's dept., foreign office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; asst. sec. to rec-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high comnr., F.M.S., and priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., May, 1907; passed in Malay and law, 1898.

SHAND, CHAS. ARTHUR.—Ed. at Harrow; mem. local assembly, Antigua, 1876-1891; ditto, federal coun., 1877-1891; J.P., Antigua, 1885; mem. ex. coun., Antigua, 1887; chmn. P.L. guardians and of several local boards, Antigua; dist. mag., Nevis, Feb., 1903; and offl. mem. leg. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis; mem. exec. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, Oct., 1904; J.P., Nov., 1904; chmn. of agric. and comm'l soc'y. of Nevis.

SHARE, HAMNET HOLDITCH.—B. 1864; entd. R. Navy, 1880; served in H.M.S. "Tourmaline" and numerous other ships (Egypt, medal, 1882, and Khedive's Bronze Star); sec. to comdr.-in-chief, Pacific station, 1899-1900; ditto, Australia station, 1901-1903; priv. sec. to gov. of N. S. Wales, May, 1903; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 22nd Nov., 1904.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. consnr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy comsnr., 1st Apr., 1896; and again acted as comsnr. and consul-gen., 16th Apr., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; comsnr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; reed. Jubilee medal, 1897; comsnr., commodr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; reed. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907.

SHARPE, W. S., C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1860; capt. 4th batt. R.I. Rifles, 1883; major, Oct., 1903; inspr., Sierra Leone frontier force, Mar., 1894; 3rd comsnr. to Anglo-French bndry. comsnr. and delimited frontier from Atlantic coast to Little Mola River, 1896; dist. comsnr., Karene dist., Jan., 1897; served in the S. Leone rebellion 1898-9 (despatches and medal); F.R.G.S., and J.P. for the col.; transf'd. to N. Nigeria as asst. res. Dec., 1900; apptd. 2nd class res. to Kontagora Prov., Oct., 1901; in Dakkakari expdn., N. Nig., 1904 (medal); in Dareng expdn., N. Nig., 1906 (dangerously wounded); in Dakkakari expdn., N. Nig., 1908.

SHAW, CHAS. COURTENAY.—B. 1878; elk. in off. of sec. to Transvaal adminisn., Mar., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to ditto, Mar. to June, 1902; priv. sec. to col. sec., Transvaal, July, 1902; ag. sec., Transvaal imgrn. offr., May, 1904, to June, 1906; J.P., Witwatersrand, May, 1906; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1st July, 1906; priv. sec. to gov., O.R.C., July, 1907.

SHAWE, HENRY BENJAMIN.—Elk. at Clanwilliam, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; col. sec.'s off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sen. examnr. of acctnts., 14th Apr., 1890; chief ditto, 1st July, 1894; acctnt., 10th July, 1895; acctntg. offr., 31st Mar., 1899; ch. elk. and acctng. offr., 1st Mar., 1911; ag. under col. sec., 1st Apr. to 30th Sept., 1901; asst. under col. sec., 1st Apr., 1902.
SHEA, THE HON. SIR E. D., KT. BACH. (1902).—Col. sec. and elk. of coun., Newfoundland, Jan., 1874; cashier of savings bank, and pres. of legis. coun., Feb., 1886.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H.A., Ferryland (Newfdld.), 1889-93; again in 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; mem. of treas. bd., 1898; mayor of St. John's, 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1904.

SHENTON, THE HON. SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1883), J.P.—B. 1842; mem. of the legislature of W. Australia, 1870-74, 1875-90; on the introduction of respon. govt., col. sec. in the first ministry formed by Sir John Forrest; pres. of the legis. coun., 1892-1900; has been mayor of Perth on eleven different occasions.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B. Ch., Cantab.—B. 1871; civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898, to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Stiltns, 1st Mar., 1901; spec. mission to Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1905.

SHEPSTONE, ARTHUR JESSE, C.M.G. (1902).—J.P. (Natal).—Asst. commr. and res. mag., Lower Umfolosi dist., Natal, 21st June, 1887; mag., Nkandhla dist., 28th Feb., 1891; Entonjaneni dist., 1st Dec., 1894; Vryheid, 27th Jan., 1903; Newcastle, 21st Nov., 1904.

SHEPSTONE, H. C., C.M.G.
