WILLIAMS, JOSHUA STRANGE, M.A.—B. 1837; ed. at Harrow and Trim Coll.; 1st class in law tripes, 1858; jun. of chancellor's legal studies medallist, 1858; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1859; dist. land agent, Canterbury, N.Z., 1871; regis. gen. of land, Land Transfer Act, 1872; puisne judg., ct. of N. Zealand, Mar., 1875; is chancellor, Univ. of Otago.
WILLIAMS, MYLES.—Clk. to res. comnr., Prot., 1902; ch. clk. and regnr., 3rd Feb., 1907.

WILLIAMS, SIR RALPH CHAMPNEY, K.T. (1907); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1848; Kossai; explored parts of Patagonia and del Fuego, 1873-4; and Cent. Africa, 1884; civil intell. dept., Bechuanaland, 1884; author of "The British Lion in Bechuanaland"; Br. consul offr., S. African Leg., 1887; Br. agt. there (with letter of cred.), 1888; col. treas., and mem. of sanitary com., Gibraltar, 1890; also contract offr., 1891; also cap. of the port, 1895; recd. cred. med. from the Italian govt. for service connection with the wreck of the "Utopia," 1891; col. sec., Barbados, 1897; res. Bechuanaland Protec., 1901; govr., Windhoek, 8th Sept., 1906.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM.—B. 1856; mun. water wks., Cyprus, 1878; surveying on military roads and draughtsman and supt of wks. under R.E. offrs. when civil wks. were under their charge; draughtsman, chief storekeeper, and asst. supt. of wks. under civil wks. dept., 2nd divisional engnr., Dec., 1898, and 1st divisional engnr., 1903; ag. offr. in charge of pub. wks. dept. on three occasions, and ag. D.P.W. on two occasions of three to six months' duration.
WILLIAMS, W. R.—Med. offr., Dist. C, Dominica, Jan., 1881.

WILLIAMSON, GEORGE ALEXANDER, M.A., M.B., M.D., Aberd. Univ., D.T.M.—B. 1871; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1895; membre correspondant de la Société de Médecine de Gand, Belgium, 1901; dip. trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool, 1900; certif. in trop. med. (with distinction), Liverpool, 1900 and 1906; passed lower standard Greek, 1900; author of several articles in British Medical Journal and Journal of Trop. Med., and a "Report on Malaria in Cyprus," 1903.

WILLIAMSON, VICTOR ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1882).—Ed. at Westminster, and Chr. Ch., Oxford, of which he is M.A.; barrister-at-law of the In. Tem., and goes the N.E. circuit; one of the royal comsrs. who in 1872 and 1873 inquired into the treatment of Indian imigrts. in Mauritius; served for more than two years in Fiji without remuneration as chmn. of comsn. for settling titles to land; was mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1879-81; sec. to roy. coman. on London bd. of wks., 1888.

WILLIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER, Sc.D.—B. 1868; Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; held for 3 years from 1890 the Frank Smart studentship for botan. research; was sen. asst. to regius prof. of botany, Glasgow Univ., and lecturer in botany, Queen Margaret's Coll., Glasgow; dir. of royal bot. gardens, Ceylon, Aug., 1896; on special duty to Fed. Malay States, Mar., 1904; organising vice-pres. of Ceylon agric. soc'y.; author of "A Manual and Dictionary of the Flowering Plants and Ferns," and numerous scientific and economic papers; editor of "Annals of the Royal Botanic Gardens, Peradeniya," "Circulars and Agricultural Journal of Royal Botanic Gardens, Ceylon," and of the "Tropical Agriculturist."

WILSON, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1895).—B. 1838; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond; home civ. serv., 1856-61; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, 1861-6; special comsnr. to report upon the salmon fisheries of N.B.; capt. and adjt. 1st York militia, N.B., 1863; also at camp of instruction, 1863-6; major, St. John volr. batt. when on service on the American frontier, 1866; priv. sec. to gov. of Trinidad, 1866-9; supt. of prisons, 1869-70; stip. mag. E. dist. of St. George, 1870-8; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain and W. dist. of St. George, Sept., 1873-4; comsnr. of the N. prov. and sub-intendant of crown lands, 1878 to 1897; mem. of exec. coun., 1894; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, various dates from Apr., 1875, to Jan., 1897; raised and organised Trinidad volr. force, 1879 to 1890; col. comdg. L.I. volrs., 1890-7, and is now hon. col.; gov. of Br. Honduras, Jan., 1891; ret. May, 1903.

WILSON, DAVID JAMES.—Seconded by Indian Govt. to Imperial Brit. E. Africa Co. as asst. of telegrapha, 1889; collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1895; chief supt. of transport and registr. of porters, 1898; dir. of Uganda transport, and in charge of depot stores, King's African Rifles (Uganda), 1899; dir. of transport, E. Africa Prot.; coastagent, Uganda Prot.; medal, Mazrui rebellion, 1895-96.
WILSON, ERNEST GEORGE.—Ed. at Whitgift Coll.; G.N. rly., Apr., 1894, to June, 1901; asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., June, 1901.

WILSON, HON. FRANK.—B. 1859; mem. of city coun., Perth, W. Australia, 1895-98; M.L.A. for Canning, 1897, and for Perth, 1901; min. for rlys. and mines, 1901, defeated on seeking re-election; M.L.A. for Sussex, 1904; min. of wks., Aug., 1905; col. treas., min. for educn. and agric., May, 1906.
WILSON, GEORGE, C.B. (1899).—Sub-comsnr., Uganda Prot., 30th Aug., 1894; dep. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1902.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 19-3; ag. asst. postmtr.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in Penang.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripos. and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry comsnr., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriages Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal asst. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal asst. on high comsnr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lient.-gov., Apr. to Aug., 19 3, Dec., 1903, to Aug., 1904, and from Aug. to Dec., 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (represented O.R.C. on rly. comtee.).

WILSON, JOHN PLOWRIGHT.—Entd. serv. of Uganda Prot. from Imperial Brit. E. Africa Co. as asst. collr., 1st Apr., 1893; collr., 1st Aug., 1895; sub-comsnr., 29th July, 1899; I. B. E. A. Co.'s medal, Uganda, 1891-2; East and Central Africa medal, Uganda, 1897-8.

WILSON, LESLIE ORME.—R.M.L.I.; joined Royal Marines as 2nd lieut., 1st Feb., 1895; lieut., 1st Jan., 1896; capt., 1st Apr., 1901; served in H.M.S. "Hibernia" and H.M.S. "Australia"; asst. instructor of musketry, Gravesend, 1901; served with naval brigade in South Africa, 1899-1900 (ment. in desp., D.S.O., medal with five clasps); A.D.C. to gov. of N. S. Wales, 12th July, 1903.

WILSON, T.—B. 1864; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the G.P.O., Apr., 1882; transf. to the C.O., Dec., 1883; ast. regist., July, 1890; higher grade, Jan., 1898.

WILSON, T. R.—Ck. to comsnr. of crown lands, co. of Bourke, Victoria, Dec., 1852; ch. clk., med. dept., Mar., 1855; sec. to cent. board of health, Aug., 1863; ch. clk., ch. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; under-sec. Victoria, 1881; J.P., 1883; hon. mem., Br. Med. Assoc., 1890.
WILSON, WILLIAM.—Comdr., ss. "Henry Wright," Imperial Brit. E. Africa Co., Oct., 1846, to Apr., 1891; comdr., ss. "Juba," E. Africa Prot., 3rd Dec., 1896.

WINDHAM, WM.—Ck., rec. comsnr.'s office, Zulu native reserve, Apr., 1883; studt. interp. native affairs office, Natal, Mar., 1884; ck. and registr. to special comsnr. for Zulu affairs, Oct., 1885; clk. and interp. to res. commn. and ch. mag., Zululand, June, 1887; sec. to ditto, Jan., 1889; clk., exec. coun., Natal, Sept., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Natal, Oct., 1889; sec. for Zululand, Dec., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Zululand, Feb., 1890; gov't. sec., Zululand, Feb., 1894; asst. under-sec. for Zululand affairs, 1897; regis. of deeds and regis.-gen., Natal, Mar., 1898; (compiled abstract of opinions and court decisions upon transactions in deeds office, 1878-1898); sec. to native affairs dept., Transvaal, 23rd Sept., 1901; M.L.C., 1905.

WINGFIELD, SIR EDWARD, K.C.B. (1899), C.B. (1889).—B. 1834; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford, B.C.L. and M.A. (Oxon); called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, 10th June, 1859; went the home circuit; apptd. asst. under-S. of S. for Cols., 19th July, 1878; permanent under-S. of S., 1st Mar., 1897, to 28th Feb., 1900; sec. to Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1897-1900.

WINGFIELD, MAURICE E.—Ed. at Winchester and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A. 1896; sec. to royal comsn., hut tax, S. Leone, 1898; ag. treas., collr. of cust., postm., and mem. ex. and leg. couns., Falkland Is., 1899-1900; priv. sec. to gov'. of Queensland, 1902; priv. sec. to gov-gen. of Australia, 1903-4; asst. sec., S. African constitutions comte., 1906.

WINKFELD, JOHN, M.A. (Oxon) (Worcester Coll.)—B. 1862; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1889; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1896; ag. queen's advocate, July, 1898, to May, 1899; rlyw. comsnr., Aro, Jan.-July, 1900; ag. atty.-gen., July-Sept., 1900; atty.-gen., So. Nig., Dec., 1900; compiled laws of So. Nig., 1900-1901; puisne judge, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. just., Lagos, Jan., 1905; ditto, Calabar, Aug., 1905, to Jan., 1906; ditto, Lagos, May to Oct., 1906; ditto, Calabar, Apr. to July, 1907; ditto, Lagos, July to Dec., 1907.
WINSTONE-WATERS, EDWARD JOHN, F.R. Phys. Soc.—Med. offr., Uganda rly., 10th May, 1898; Uganda Prot., 10th Dec., 1900; transf'd to E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1902.

WINTER, SIR FRANCIS PRATT, KT. (1900), C.M.G.(1892).—B. 1848; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, 1887-88; ch. judicial offr. and mem. exec. and legis. comns., Br. N. Guinea, 1888; ag. admin., Sept., 1894, to June, 1895, and Oct., 1895, to Mar., 1899; ret., 1903.

WINTER, SIR J. S., K.C.M.G. (1888), K.C.—B. 1840; speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1877-8; mem. exec. coun., Feb., 1879; solr.-gen., Oct., 1882; atty.-gen., 1886; agt. for Newfoundland at fishery confce., Washington, 1887; regis. office of atty.-gen., 1889; puisne judge, 1893; resig. 1896; prime min., 1897-1900; atty.-gen., 1897; mem. of delegn. to imperial gov't. re French treaties and other matters, 1898; min. of just. and mem. of treas. bd., 1898; high comsnr. to internat. confce. between Gt. Brit. and U.S.A. at Quebec and Washington, 1898.

WISE, ALFRED GASCOYNE, LL.B.—Ed. at Repton, at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1878; clk. to ch. just. at Hong Kong, 1882 (resig., 1883); pol. mag., Jan., 1884; ag. comsnr. in addition, Apr. to Dec., 1884; ag. regist. of sup. ct., official trustee; regist. of companies, and land offr., Mar., 1885, regist. sup. ct., 1892; ag. puisne judge, Jan., 1889, to Sept., 1890; discharged duties of two pol. mags. and coroner, March, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. puisne judge for two months in 1893; ag. puisne judge from Apr., 1894, to Apr., 1895; ag. puisne judge, Jan. to Apr., 1895; ag. atty-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1895; puisne judge, Hong Kong; 1895; ag. ch. just., June, 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. gen., Apr. to June, 1902; chairman, sq' board, Aug., 1902; acted for ch. just., Sept., Oct., 1905; ag. ch. just., Sept., 1907.
WISE, BERNHARD RINGROSE, K.C.—Gen., N.S. Wales, May, 1887, to Feb., 1888; 1901, and 1901-4; mem. of Austn. Fed. 1897-8.
WITHERS, A. K.—Ed. at Bedford served in survey dept. under Gov't of Eg' confdl. clk. to admstr. of Gambia, 1897.

WITTENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1834; elected M.L.C., W. Aust., 1883; resig.in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig.; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, M.L.C.

WODEHOUSE, CLARENCE B.—Ed. at Hurst Coll., 1875; gazetted to 77th Regt.; resigned coman., 1880; local commdt. of Cyprus, 1st Jan., 1894; ag. comanr., Limassol, 12th July, 1894; ditto, Papho, 1st Apr., 1895; comanr., Papho, 7th June, 1895.

WODEHOUSE, H.E., C.M.G. (1886).—Ed. Repton Schl.; Hong Kong cadet, 1867; interp., 1869; sup. of Chinese pol., 1870; col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1875; pol. mag., hon. sec. to the local coman. in connection with the Indian and Col. Exhib., and special ex' in London, 1886; mem. exec. coun., May, 1887; retired.

WODEHOUSE, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH HENRAGE, K.C.B. (1888) C.M.G.—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comd. R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov't. of Eng., 1887-1894; comdd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1898; with Malakand field force (wounded, 1898); in action with Egyptian force, Argin; infantry div. at Toski; President, 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Co., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); So. War (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osman-Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, Fr Gov't; gov. of Bermuda, 30th May, 1907.

WODEHOUSE, P. P. J.—Ed. Eliot Co., Guernsey; first clk., reg.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; in charge of census, Dec., 1900; asst. supt. of police, attached Punjab pol., 1901-2; passed in U.P., 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1907; ag. supt. of pol. and fire brig., 1907.

WOLFE, E. D. C.—Tonbridge Schl. (1890); exhib. in mod. langa., Caius Coll., Camb.; 1st class in mod. langa., tripods. (1896); 1st class Pt. I., 2nd class Pt. II., Law Special (1897); B.A. (1898); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to learn Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1900; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; regist., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1902; ag. regist.-gen., Nov., 1902; sec. to pub. wks., coman., 1902; collr. of crown rent, New Terri., Feb., 1903; regist., land ct., Mar., 1903; ag. mag., N.T., and asst. supt. of pol., 1903-4; collr. of crown rent, N.T., 1904; seconded to Trans- gov't serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Apr. and Nov., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907; ag. regist.-gen., Aug., 1907; asst. regist.-gen., Oct., 1907.

WOLFERSSTAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PHILIP.—Ed. Eton and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1889; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smi... Jan. to Apr., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and regir. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; offl. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903; sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1907; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1907.

WOLFF, ERNEST CHARTERIS HOLFOORD.—F.M.S. civ. ser.; b. 1875; ed. at Merchiston and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1897; cadet, Pahang, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1899; ag. treas., supt. of posts and teles., and collr. of stamp duties, Pahang, Jan., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, May, 1899; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, May, 1901; sec. to ditto, Oct., 1901; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1903; dist. treas., Telok Anson, Jan., 1905, but still ag. sec. to res., N. Semb.; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1905; ag. asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1906.

WOLLASTON, HARRY NEWTON PHILLIPS, C.M.G. (1907); I.S.O. (1903); LL.D., J.P.—B. 1846; apptd. (after exam.), to dept. of trade and custs., 1863; grad. with hons., in law, Melb. Univ., 1884; called to the bar, 1884, and apptd. counsel to marine bd., Victoria, which apptnt. he held till 1901; promoted to bech. elk. of dept., 1899; apptd. permanent head of Victorian dept. of trade and cust., 1891; compt.-gen. of Commonwealth dept., of trade and cust., and permanent head, 1901; represented Australia at Impl. Shipping Conference, London, 1907.

WOLSELEY, FIELD-MARSHAL VISCOUNT (created 1885), SIR GARNET JOSEPH WOLSELEY, G.C.B. (1880), K.C.M.G. (1870), G.C.M.G. and K.C.B. (1874), BARON (1882), K.P. (1885).—B. 1832; ensign, Mar., 1852; capt., Jan., 1855; major, 90th foot, Mar., 1858; lieut.-col., Apr., 1859; col., June, 1865; maj.-gen., 1874; lieut.-gen., Apr., 1878; gen. 1882; field-marshal, 1895; served with 80th foot in the Burmese war of 1852-53, severely wounded (medal); served with distinction in the Crimea and taking of Sebastopol, with the 90th L.I., and was severely wounded in a sortie, after which he received the Legion of Honour, the 5th class of the Medjidie, etc.; was also at the siege and capture of Lucknow and defence of Alumagh, and numerous actions in Oudh, when he was made brevet lieut.-col.; served in China on the staff of the qtrmr.-gen. throughout the Chinese campaign of 1860, for which he also received a medal and two clasps; dep. qtrmr.-gen. in Canada, Oct., 1867; asst. adjt.-gen. at headqrs., 1871; apptd. for special service on the G. Coast in connection with the Ashantee war, Aug., 1873; brought the Ashantee war to a successful conclusion, and received the thanks of the House of Lords and the House of Commons for his services; was made a G.C.M.G. and a K.C.B., and a major-gen.; and inspr.-gen. of auxiliary forces, Apr., 1874; specially apptd. to administer temporarily the govt. of Natal from Feb. to Sept., 1875; acted as vice-pres. of the London S. African confce., Aug., 1876; mem. of the Indian coun., 1876; comnrn. and comdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, July, 1878; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Natal, and of the Transvaal, and comdr. of the forces in S. Africa, May, 1879, and high comnrn. for S. E. Africa; qtrmr.-gen., Apr., 1880; adjt.-gen., Mar., 1882; commanded the forces in Egypt, with the temporary rank of gen., in 1882, and received the thanks of both houses of parlmt., and a peacock; and from the Khedive of Egypt the Grand Order of the Osmanieh; commanded the Nile expdn., and other operations in Egypt and the Soudan for the relief of Gen. Gordon, 1884-5; received the thanks of both houses of parl.; was raised to the rank of viscount, and made K.P.; is author of "The Soldier's Pocket Book for Field Service"; comdr.-in-ch. in Ireland, 1890; comdr.-in-ch., 1895-1900.
WOOD, A. E.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907.

WOOD, DAVID.—Ed. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1891; supt. of accts., corres., and stores, P.W.D., 1894; was sec. to comnrn. on housing the Chinese during the plague, 1894; ag. govt. assessor of taxes, July, 1900, to Nov., 1901; May to Oct., 1902; and from Oct., 1905, to Oct., 1906; J.P., 1902.

WOOD, FIELD-MARSHAL SIR HENRY EVELYN, V.C., G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1838; entered the navy 1852, and the army 1855; served in the Crimea; received the V.C. for his gallantry in hunting down the rebels in the Seronge jungles; admstd. the govt. of Natal, from Feb. to Dec., 1881; commanded the forces in Natal and the Transvaal, and was a mem. of the roy. comnrn. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881, but dissented from the report; commanded the 2nd brigade, 2nd div., in Egypt, 1882; apptd. by the Khedive to the command of the Egyptian troops, 1883; served in the Nile expdn., 1884; comdr. of the E. dist., 1889; qtrmr.-gen. of the forces, Oct., 1893; apptd. to command of army corps, 1901.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripos, 1899; B.A., 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; registr. of land ct., Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, May to July, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4; asst. land offr. in N.T., Jan., 1905; ag. asst. reg.-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., May, 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Mar., 1908.
WOOD, JOHN T.—M.A. (Oxon); F.R.G.S.; registrar of mining rights, Transvaal.
WOODARD, HENRY.—Govt. storekeeper, B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1893; ch. commissariat offr., 1894; ch. transport offr., 1906.

WOODCOCK, GEORGE ALBERT.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Battersea; apptd. asst. master Victoria Coll., Hong Kong, 15th Mar., 1890; final bar, Gray's Inn, 1897; ag. sec., sanitary bd., Mar., 1900, to July, 1901; sec. of san. bd., July, 1901; J.P., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 5th July, 1905.

WOODE, BENJAMIN TREGASKIS, M.R.P.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Wesleyan High school, Freetown, S. Leone; jun. clk., Queen's advocate off., S. Leone, Apr., 1890, to July, 1891; cust. offr., Forcados, Benin and Warri dists., in Oil Rivers Prot., Aug., 1891, to July, 1892; cust. offr., Bonny and New Calabar dists., July, 1892, to Aug., 1892; postmr., Bonny, N.C.P., from Aug., 1892, to Nov., 1894; beachmaster, storekeeper, and supervisor of workmen at Bonny and Cawthorne channel, Jan., 1893, to Nov., 1894; transf. to Old Calabar, to take up duty there, Mar., 1895; postmr., Brass, May, 1895, to Apr., 1899; postmr., Opobo, Apr., 1899,
WOODFORD, CHAS. MORRIS.—B. 1853; ed. at Tonbridge schl.; clk. in rec.-gen.'s off., Fiji, 1883; ag. consul, Samoa, 1895; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 1895; res. commr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1897.
WOODS, HENRY J. B.—Mem. exec. coun. and survr.-gen., Newfoundland, 1889-1893, and from 1895 to 1897; mem. exec. coun., 1900; postmr.-gen., 1902.

WOODS, PERCY SINCLAIR COMYN.—Ed. St. Michael's Schl., Lyme Regis, and Durham Univ.; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Somerset House, 1st Jan., 1903; asst. auditor, Lagos and S. Nigeria, 29th Apr., 1905.

WOODS, WILFRID WESTWORTH.—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schol., mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 2nd Sept., 1901; asst. auditor, G. Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1904.

WOODWARD, LIONEL MABBT.—B. 1864; ed. at Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb. (scholar); 1st class honours, classical tripes; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1892; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Tamil, July, 1890; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag. and asst. Indian immigr. agt., Singapore, July, 1892; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1893; sheriff and dep. regisr. sup. ct., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. offl. assignee, S. Stiltna., and regisr. of deeds, Apr., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. sen. dist. offr., Prov. Well., Oct., 1900; asst. regisr., supreme court, Penang, May, 1902; ag. solr.-gen., May, 1903, dep. pub. prosor., Mar., 1904; seconded for spec. serv. in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., May, 1905; judl. commr., F.M.S. Jan., 1906.

WRAGG, SIR W. T., KT. R.ACH. (1891), M.A., D.C.L., Oxon. (1894).—B. 1842; open classical exhib. Lincl. Coll., Oxon, Apr., 1863, and open classical scholarship at Worcester Coll., May, 1863; B.A., 1stclass honours, 1867; mem. of the In. Tem., Apr., 1866; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1868; commr., of requests, Harrispattu, July, 1868; in charge of the office of the dir. of pub. instruction, Mar., 1869; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1869; pol. mag., Panwila, 1869; pol. mag. of Gampola, Pussellawa, and Nawalapitya, 1871; dist. judge, Chilaw, June, 1872; called to the bar by the In. Tem., Jan., 1879; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1879; ditto, Badulla, 1879; puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, July, 1883; pres. of comm. to inquire into the working of the Ind. immigr. laws in Natal, 1885-7; pres. of special judicial comm. for trial of Zulu chiefs, 1888-9; ret., 1898.

WRAY, CHAS. JAMES HILL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Surgeon, immigr. service, Queensland, 1876; health offr., Brisbane, 1879; med. offr. penal establt., St. Helena, and boys' reformatory, Lytton, and visiting surg. benevolent asylum, Dunwich, 1879; inspr. of orphanages, 1882; sec. to central bd. of health, 1884.

WRIGHT, ERIC BLACKWOOD.—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin (B.A., 1882); 1st jun. mod. in history, polit. econ. and law and silver medallist); LL.B. (1st cls. in honours); LL.D. (stip. cond.) prizeman in German and French; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1885; holder of two 1st scholarships of 100 guineas each respectively, and a 2nd scholarship of 100 guineas at the Middle Temple; prizeman of legal educn.; ch. just., Seychelles, 1892-1905; admst. govt., May to Nov., 1907; author of "Wright's Law of Principal and Agent," edit., 1893, 2nd edit., 1901; and the 2nd ed. of "Saunders on Negligence"; author of a translation with notes of the French Civil Code.

WRIGHT, G. H. BATESON.—Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1875; Denver theological schol.; Nicott Hebrew schol., 1876; Houghton Syrac. Pusey and Ellerton Hebrew schol., 1877-8; D.D., 1891; apptd. headm. of College, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1881; as inst. in schls., Feb. to Dec., 1883; author of "Translation of Book of Job"; "Was Israel in Egypt?" and various school books; 1885.

WRIGHT, HON. J. ARTHUR, C.E., M.I.C.E.—Comsr. of rly., dir. of pub. works engrnr.-in-ch., mem. of legis. coun. and exec. of W. Australia, 1885-90; temporary ad. internal and cust. revenue at Albany, W.A., Nov., 1897; ag. govt. res., Albany, Jan., 1898; govt. res., Apr., 1899.

WRIGHT, REGINALD BARTON.—Assoc. Guilds cent. instn. engng.; late asst. trigonometrical survey, Perak, Malay Penins., late asst. engrnr., Uganda rly.; chief engrnr., Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1903; land offr., May, 1903.
WRIGHT, WM.—B. 1859; clk. to pub. trust., S. Aust., 1885; clk. to pub. trust., clk., succession duties dept., 1891; pub. trustee and curator of convicts estates, 1903.

WRIGHTSON, WALSH, C.M.G., M.I.C.E.—B. 1852; served articles with Fowler, Leeds; asst. to boro. engineer, Leeds dist. engineer, P.W.D., Ceylon, 1875; seen serv. on Nanuova Rly. extensions, 1888; serv. to restore Kalawana irrigation tank; on successful completion, promoted prov. N.W. Prov., 1888; asst. to dir. pub. wks., prov. engr., E. Prov., 1894; dir. pub. wks., Trinidad, and oth. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1895; apptd. mem. ex. coun., June, 1895; res. harbour, water and sewerage wks., 1896-1907.

WRIXON, THE HON. SIR HENRY K.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1839; atty.-gen., Van. Feb., 1866; resign., Nov., 1890; mem. of Feder. Confce., 1891; represented Victoria at Col. 1894; author of "Socialism: Notes on a Pacific Tour."
WROUGHTON, LEWIS.—Sub.-insp. of land mtd. pol., 1884; ag. asst. commr., Queens., 1890; asst. commr., Basutoland, 1891; sec., 1901; govt. sec., 1902.

WYATT, ROBERT.—B. 1870; Lond. Metrop. 1891 to 1895; B. Hond. pol., June, 1895; supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902 to 1903; ag. dist. commr., Orange Walk, Aug., 1903; ag. dist. commr., Coroal, 2nd to 11th July, 1905; ditto, Caye, Feb., 1906; supt., pol., 14th July, 1905; J.P. for Coroal; Hythe extra certif., 1899; supt. of pol., Ist, 1906; certif. of proficiency for field offr., spec. distinction, schl. of instr., Chelsea bar, 1907; certif. for management of English com. and local prisons, 1907; attached to London brigade for training, 1907.

WYLIE, THOS. ALEXANDER.—B. 1866; traffic supt., Ceylon rly., 17th May, 1888; traffic supt., on several occasions; ag. ass't. 27th Apr., 1893; acctnt., 11th May, 1894; traffic supt., 1st Feb., 1906; ag. gen. man., 16th Apr., 1907.
WYLLY, EDWARD—Police probationer, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1904.
WYNNE, CHAS. OWEN, L.R.C.P. and S.E.—B. 1871; dist. med. offr., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., May, 1903; ag. comanr., mag., and med. offr., Virgin Is., May, 1906; dist. med. offr., Nevis, Feb., 1907.

YOUNG, ALFRED KARNEY.—B.A., Magd. Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. registr. gen., 1895-7; dist. comanr., Orange Walk, 1898; registr. gen., June, 1898; ag. suptdt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mar., 1906.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON, K.C.M.G. (1908): C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. pol., Kyrena, Aug., 1878; asst. comanr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comanr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admtd. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connection with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec., S. Sttlmts., June, 1906.
YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED.—Ed. at Derby Schl. and Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906.

YOUNG, ROBT. HEYDEN.—B. 1858; sub-survr., survey dept., S. Sttlmts., 28th Jan., 1878; computer and draftsman, Malacca, 1st May, 1881; dist. survr., Oct., 1882; ch. survr., 12th July, 1888; senior survr., Jan., 1891; senior survr. and supervisor of survey schl., 1902; suptdg. re-survey of Singapore, 4th May, 1903; senior survey offr., Dec., 1903, but cont. to act as supt. of re-survey and supervisor of survey schl.

YOUNG, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of B. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. govt. sec., B. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comanr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admnstr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906.
